_\---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Greg Casar
Date: Wed, Feb 22, 2023
Subject: Supporting workers on strike in Texas_
Bernie Sanders and I are teaming up to defend workers’ rights in Texas, Peter.
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59185532/393496124/-803653937?v=MkEf8l8MNMs&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85NDI5NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjNDUwMzBiNi01ZWI0LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=T6gpC_wR6aaDf6U3o0YqQEbig48STcAL6Y0b5tus02k=&emci=d7a4b1d1-5ab4-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&emdi=c45030b6-5eb4-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&ceid=6830379)
This past fall, dozens of YouTube Music workers in Austin and across the country filed a petition to create a union. These workers often make $19/hour, even while Google makes billions in profits. In an apparent attempt to retaliate against the union drive, Cognizant, the Google contractor that employs the YouTube workers, issued a “return-to-office mandate.” However, many of the YouTube workers have second jobs as touring musicians, or live outside or Austin, and now face possible termination because of this mandate.
To make matters worse, after the workers went on strike to protest the union-busting, Cognizant doubled-down and has reportedly offshored their work to workers in India.
**Yesterday Senator Bernie Sanders and I sent a letter to Alphabet and Google CEO Sundar Pichai urging him to ensure all Google workers can exercise their right to join a union.** As we said in our letter, _a company with $60 billion in profits last year can afford to treat their workers fairly._
As a long-time labor organizer, I know how important it is to have allies in these fights. That’s why Bernie and I are working to build a movement to deliver better protections, pay, and benefits for workers like those in Austin — from passing the PRO Act, to guaranteeing paid sick and family leave, to strengthening rights of workers to unionize.
Peter, it’s critical that we speak out when companies use their power and size to retaliate against workers trying to unionize.
You can rest assured that Bernie and I will continue to use our voices to speak out against any reported union-busting and ensure that all workers are able to freely exercise their right to join a union and earn a decent wage.
Thank you for having our backs,
— Greg
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59185533/393496125/-1865831370?refcode=em-20230224&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85NDI5NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjNDUwMzBiNi01ZWI0LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=T6gpC_wR6aaDf6U3o0YqQEbig48STcAL6Y0b5tus02k=)
_Congressman Greg Casar
Texas Congressional District 35_

name: LTC (Ret.) Alex Vindman
subject: Ukraine’s fight is our fight
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY VOTEVETS, VOTEVETS
body:
Tell Congress that you stand in support of Ukraine
#
Alex Vindman here.
It's been one year since Putin's lawless, unprovoked invasion of Ukraine.
Thousands of Ukrainians have been killed. Many thousands more injured. Millions more have been displaced.
While Trump was in office, his corruption emboldened Putin, weakened Ukraine, and undermined U.S. national security. **I witnessed Trump’s corruption firsthand.** And if you look back to 2021, it was in the weeks and months after Trump’s insurrection that Putin began building up his forces along Ukraine’s border. It’s because of Trump’s cheerleading that Putin thought he could get away with his illegal war.
Putin thought the United States was weak. That we’d let him get away with attacking Ukraine.
But he was wrong.
Thanks to the resolve of the Ukrainian military and its people, our NATO allies, and the leadership of President Biden, Ukraine held on. Because of our support to Ukraine, Russia will cease to be a conventional military threat to NATO and the U.S. for the next decade.
And it’s important we’re clear here: There is no getting back to normal with Russia. Not without victory for Ukraine. The best-case scenario for our long-term national security is to help the Ukrainians liberate their territory and beat Putin.
But the far-right doesn’t see it that way.
From Trump to Tucker Carlson to Marjorie Taylor Greene to Matt Gaetz, they’re all too happy to pander to and cheerlead for Putin. For a full year, they’ve flooded this country with lies and disinformation on Putin’s behalf. Far-right radicals are even pushing “Ukraine Fatigue” bills in Congress, trying to limit the aid we can provide.
Lies, division, and disinformation compromise our national security and help Putin. We can’t let that happen. We have to show Congress that the American people are united behind Ukraine. VoteVets certainly is, and so am I.
[**So, I’m asking: Add your name here and tell Congress that you stand in support of Ukraine. Exactly one year on, there has never been a better time.**](https://act.votevets.org/signup/230224_vv_ukraine_petition/?source=em230224-1-15&refcode2=7739.3013755.Ed3s3T&t=1&akid=7739%2E3013755%2EEd3s3T)
Ukraine’s fight is our fight. I hope you'll add your name.
Onward to victory. Sláva Ukrayíni!
LTC (Ret.) Alexander Vindman
| [ADD YOUR NAME](https://act.votevets.org/signup/230224_vv_ukraine_petition/?source=em230224-1-15&refcode2=7739.3013755.Ed3s3T&t=2&akid=7739%2E3013755%2EEd3s3T)
---

Candidates like me exist in states, counties, and communities across the nation -- but they need help from grassroots supporters like you. | |

Derek, last month, I was incredibly honored to be sworn in as the first Democratic Gen Z member of Congress. I am proud to be of a generation that is ready to claim our seat at the table, fight for progress, and build a better future that takes on gun violence, rising health care costs, and climate change. But I cannot be an anomaly. Candidates like me exist in states, counties, and communities across the nation. They just need the help from grassroots supporters like you, Derek, to run and to win. **That’s why I’m reaching out to you for the first time to ask that you pitch in a $7 donation to the DNC before their February end-of-month deadline. Your investment will be put to work supporting Democratic candidates and ensuring that our nation’s governing body looks like America.**

I never planned to run for office at 25. But I took the risk because I saw a problem that needed fixing and I knew that the most vulnerable needed a fighter on their side who knew their struggles. And it wasn’t easy. After months of driving for Uber on the side to make ends meet, we ultimately won. We need to get the word out to Democrats across SD-09. name: Dick Durbin
subject: One year later, Ukraine still stands
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Dick Durbin
body:
Here’s how you can help recovery efforts →
#
_“One year later, Kyiv stands. And Ukraine stands. Democracy stands.” — President Biden_
Peter, today marks one year since Russia’s invasion of Ukraine.
This week, the U.S. formally declared that Russia has committed crimes against humanity. In a historic visit to Kyiv, President Biden reaffirmed our solidarity with Ukraine’s fight for democracy.
I am working with my colleagues in the Senate to support Ukraine, but there is more we can do.
**World Central Kitchen (WCK), a non-profit led by Chef José Andrés, has been on the ground since this crisis began, providing meals and nourishment for people evacuating the war zones. Now, as more Ukrainians begin to return home, WCK is focused on providing thousands of fresh meals and meal kits for hundreds of communities across Ukraine.**
**[Donate $5 or whatever you can to help World Central Kitchen provide meals in Ukraine.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59187079/393501593/688464584?refcode=dd_em_230224_1_t1&amount=5&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&addr1=&city=Englewood&state=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODQ4OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzOGJlZTFhYy02MmI0LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=hl0KLPODvigq6snjWKZZfM5UN4fG9u80t4cfmqhLW48=)**
More than eight million Ukrainians have fled their homes in the past year. The World Central Kitchen has been committed to feeding those entering surrounding countries. Now, WCK’s Chefs for Ukraine initiative is helping communities recover by serving meals in liberated areas across the country.
The Ukrainian people have shown tremendous courage and determination in this difficult time. Let’s continue to stand with them, help them recover, and ensure families are nourished and fed.
**[Last year, this team raised nearly $13,000 for World Central Kitchen’s work in Ukraine. I know we can do it again to support their ongoing recovery efforts. Contribute $5 now.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59187080/393501594/688464584?refcode=dd_em_230224_1_t1&amount=5&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&addr1=&city=Englewood&state=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODQ4OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzOGJlZTFhYy02MmI0LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=hl0KLPODvigq6snjWKZZfM5UN4fG9u80t4cfmqhLW48=)**
My best,
Dick Durbin
**** ******** ****
********[CONTRIBUTE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59187081/393501595/688464584?refcode=dd_em_230224_1_t1&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&addr1=&city=Englewood&state=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODQ4OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzOGJlZTFhYy02MmI0LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=hl0KLPODvigq6snjWKZZfM5UN4fG9u80t4cfmqhLW48=)********

name: News Alerts via AAU
subject: RE: One Year 🇺🇦
disclaimer_text: Paid for by An America United](https://links
body:
| | | [](https://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=96b171ff8fa34eb89314b2433106dae3&_m=c8b1bddf278c44179c9fc67285411bf5&_e=WQYuRp_u-lOaldelX3atyEvMakco6iWphxCA-GzfqppL5JYzlvonLqXgiHKiBAD7PlwdtXH3wx33I4n2dED1gFG26wKrnpJN5zAX2Q2avA2_oxCIbE1VRZZ8CUUbb6xwcza7MnCVFgZR2hgkBGyJzMurkxPx7G9LU0cQcXwhEm-tZ4nYErUMrB7al9GPQHtWR15ov7ggz9JNitsH3-yfe22K4IvGMdcuIhfJndXBmBGZeHIexuj6iYelxl8Ayn1MIW4zPs1uf16j0Qk4GodRfQ%3D%3D)
---
Today marks one year since Russia invaded Ukraine, friend. For the Ukrainian people, it ignited a year of ongoing terror, fighting, death, and destruction. An America United together with Governor Larry Hogan called for the American people to stand united behind Ukraine because if we let Russia's aggression succeed, freedom will come under threat elsewhere. We know that our nation is strongest when we stand together.
---
---
_Photo: March 2, 2022 Ukraine Solidarity Vigil hosted by Governor Larry Hogan_
---
Not only is it a time to reflect on our own sovereignty and freedoms, but to do all that we can to fortify our national security and our cybersecurity to defend our country. We are dedicated to finding national unity to our foreign policy challenges, and we hope you're on board with our important mission. Thank you, _**Team An America United**_ _**P.S. **_If you agree that we need stronger leadership and common sense policies to our many challenges, join _An America United_ today and help us fight for a better path forward in America.

clean_body: | | | Today marks one year since Russia invaded Ukraine, friend. For the Ukrainian people, it ignited a year of ongoing terror, fighting, death, and destruction. An America United together with Governor Larry Hogan called for the American people to stand united behind Ukraine because if we let Russia's aggression succeed, freedom will come under threat elsewhere. We know that our nation is strongest when we stand together. Photo: March 2, 2022 Ukraine Solidarity Vigil hosted by Governor Larry Hogan Not only is it a time to reflect on our own sovereignty and freedoms, but to do all that we can to fortify our national security and our cybersecurity to defend our country. We are dedicated to finding national unity to our foreign policy challenges, and we hope you're on board with our important mission. Thank you, Team An America United P.S. If you agree that we need stronger leadership and common sense policies to our many challenges, join An America United today and help us fight for a better path forward in America. | | | | | Join An America United on social media! | | ---|--- | | | | | |

name: Jason Crow
subject: The sun continues to rise over a free Ukraine
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jason Crow for Congress
body:
Jason Crow For Congress [https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/NGP/NGP21/1/88523/images/logoJC%20(1).png]
Folks,

One year ago today, an unprecedented era of relative peace and stability in Europe came to an end.

In the dead of night, Vladimir Putin weaponized the Russian army in an unprovoked act of aggression against a sovereign democratic nation. Putin’s recklessness has caused terrible death and suffering for both the Ukrainian and Russian people.

On paper, the Russian army should have easily defeated Ukraine.

But armies don’t fight on paper. Determination, grit, skill, and courage all matter. This past year has shown that Ukranians are some of the toughest fighters in history.

It’s been 365 days now. The sun continues to rise over a free Ukraine. Not by chance, but because Ukrainians continue to resist. Fiercely. Ukrainians are fighting for every house, every block, every inch of their country. I had the tremendous honor of witnessing that bravery firsthand when visiting Kyiv.

https://news.un.org/en/focus/ukraine/donate [https://news.un.org/en/focus/ukraine/donate]

The Biden administration has been a strong partner — gathering a coalition of our allies and partners to support Ukraine. Just last month, the United States committed to sending 31 Abrams tanks to Ukraine — marking a major next step.

I am hopeful of the progress we’re making, but we must continue moving at the speed of war.

As a member of Congress serving on the House Foreign Affairs and Intelligence Committees, I’ll continue working to support our allies and ensure Ukraine has the resources it needs to win this war.

Democracy isn’t inevitable. We all have a part to play in defeating authoritarianism at home and abroad.

The United Nations is raising money to help aid Ukraine. Make a donation now to help support refugees and migrants, deliver food to Ukranian people in dire need, and more. [https://news.un.org/en/focus/ukraine/donate]

DONATE [https://news.un.org/en/focus/ukraine/donate]

Slava Ukraini!

Jason

Now more than ever, we need strong leaders like Jason working for us in Congress. Jason is fighting for you, your family, and the American people in Congress — and he's not taking a dime of corporate PAC money. Will you pitch in now to help him continue working for us?
DONATE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/crow-february-2023]
Jason Crow for Congress
8547 E Arapahoe Road, STE J-543
Greenwood Village, CO 80112
United States
www.jasoncrowforcongress.com [https://www.jasoncrowforcongress.com/] | info@jasoncrowforcongress.com [info@jasoncrowforcongress.com]

name: Claire
subject: A desperate mess
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the National Democratic Redistricting Committee, Kelly Burton, President
body:
|
---

The House GOP is a mess, team. A desperate mess.

To begin with, **Kevin McCarthy** was barely even able to muster the support to become speaker. He only prevailed after 15 grueling votes – but not before Republicans nearly devolved into a fistfight on the House floor.

Once he finally won the gavel, McCarthy awarded **Marjorie Taylor Greene** – a MAGA extremist who spread QAnon conspiracy theories and expressed sympathy for insurrectionists – with a seat on the Homeland Security Committee. Yes, _really_.

Then, as one of their first matters of business, MAGA Republicans passed **not one, but two measures restricting abortion access nationwide** , despite a majority of Americans clearly opposing this extreme act.

And now, Republicans are threatening to plunge the economy into chaos unless Congress agrees to **slash Social Security and Medicare** , even though the vast majority of Americans support these programs. **How did extremist leaders who advocate policies most Americans vehemently oppose manage to take control of the House majority?** **The answer is one word: Gerrymandering.** **[_By manipulating maps, Republicans can all but guarantee that they’ll win majority power, even if they only win a minority of votes! That’s why the NDRC is working to end gerrymandering nationwide and ensure fairness in our elections. Will you donate now to support our work?_](https://click.everyaction.com/k/59188191/393505280/-1795600110?amount=30&refcode=EM_RTI_20230224_FR_2M_redlight922_527&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImNmZDg1YmFjLTY0YjQtZWQxMS1hOGUwLTAwMjI0ODMyZTgxMSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=79NNzkDeoQ5DOA8nGb3G7tM_vhuL4EZ3QCOV8BCaBN8=)** | _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_

Recently, Attorney General Eric Holder said: “**Too many in the Republican party have made peace with the notion that they’re going to be a minority party that has majority power. They’re okay with getting fewer votes as long as they hold on to the power that they have.**”

The NDRC is _not_ okay with that, team. And that means we have an important choice to make.

We can either sit idly by while Republicans continue to use anti-democratic tactics to win unearned power, or we can continue fighting across the country to end map manipulation and defend voting rights so we can create a level playing field where Democrats can win.

clean_body: | The House GOP is a mess, team. A desperate mess. To begin with, Kevin McCarthy was barely even able to muster the support to become speaker. He only prevailed after 15 grueling votes – but not before Republicans nearly devolved into a fistfight on the House floor. Once he finally won the gavel, McCarthy awarded Marjorie Taylor Greene – a MAGA extremist who spread QAnon conspiracy theories and expressed sympathy for insurrectionists – with a seat on the Homeland Security Committee. Yes, really. Then, as one of their first matters of business, MAGA Republicans passed not one, but two measures restricting abortion access nationwide , despite a majority of Americans clearly opposing this extreme act. And now, Republicans are threatening to plunge the economy into chaos unless Congress agrees to slash Social Security and Medicare , even though the vast majority of Americans support these programs. How did extremist leaders who advocate policies most Americans vehemently oppose manage to take control of the House majority? The answer is one word: Gerrymandering. = | If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: = | = = | = = | = Recently, Attorney General Eric Holder said: “Too many in the Republican party have made peace with the notion that they’re going to be a minority party that has majority power. They’re okay with getting fewer votes as long as they hold on to the power that they have.” The NDRC is not okay with that, team. And that means we have an important choice to make. We can either sit idly by while Republicans continue to use anti-democratic tactics to win unearned power, or we can continue fighting across the country to end map manipulation and defend voting rights so we can create a level playing field where Democrats can win. = Thank you for sticking with us. Claire Director of Political & Campaigns = = Since 2017, the NDRC has executed a comprehensive redistricting strategy that shifts the redistricting power, creating fair districts where Democrats can compete. Our victories have been made possible by our strong community of grassroots supporters. = If you want to receive periodic updates from the NDRC on our fight for fair maps, text MAPS to 36787. Text HELP for help, STOP to end. Msg & Data rates may apply. They'll be throwing everything they've got at him – including high-dollar donations from dark money groups. I have seen firsthand how Jon has fought tirelessly to level the playing field for working families in Montana and across the country. **[Can you join me in supporting Jon with a donation right now?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59188885/393507440/-1705519941?refcode=2023-02-24-tester&recurring=0&amount=3&amounts=10,30,50,100,200&refcode2=d440375b-65b4-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDEvMS84OTQxMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkNDQwMzc1Yi02NWI0LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=aRqARsP6IiPElN9KdiF0T1gF_JfZ3fb5W7hY8uMY7qY=)** [Donate Now](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59188887/393507442/-1705519941?refcode=2023-02-24-tester&recurring=0&amount=3&amounts=10,30,50,100,200&refcode2=d440375b-65b4-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDEvMS84OTQxMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkNDQwMzc1Yi02NWI0LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=aRqARsP6IiPElN9KdiF0T1gF_JfZ3fb5W7hY8uMY7qY=) **This is going to be a tough race, but Jon is the right candidate for the job.** As the only member of the Montana delegation to hold regular town halls, he makes it a top priority to hear directly from his constituents about their priorities. I've worked with Jon on legislation to get dark money and special interests out of our elections. **He is a fierce advocate for our democracy.** When you make a donation to his campaign, you're not just supporting him. You're giving Jon the resources to fight back against Mitch McConnell's disastrous policies and work to protect our progress — for all of us. **[Let's show Jon we've got his back. clean_body: | | | | have some great news to share with you: my colleague and friend, Senator Jon Tester just announced that he is running for reelection in Montana. Jon has proven himself to be a steadfast leader, fighting to protect our public lands, create good-paying jobs, and ensure rural communities have hospitals within driving distance in case of an emergency. I'll be honest with you, Jon is also one of the most vulnerable Democrats up for reelection next year. Republicans would love to take this seat and the Senate majority. They'll be throwing everything they've got at him – including high-dollar donations from dark money groups. I have seen firsthand how Jon has fought tirelessly to level the playing field for working families in Montana and across the country. = = This is going to be a tough race, but Jon is the right candidate for the job. As the only member of the Montana delegation to hold regular town halls, he makes it a top priority to hear directly from his constituents about their priorities. I've worked with Jon on legislation to get dark money and special interests out of our elections. He is a fierce advocate for our democracy. When you make a donation to his campaign, you're not just supporting him. You're giving Jon the resources to fight back against Mitch McConnell's disastrous policies and work to protect our progress — for all of us. = Ron | Express Donate: If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, we'll process your contribution instantly: | = | = | = | = ---|---|---|--- | | Paid for and authorized by Wyden for Senate. | This email was sent by Wyden for Senate P.O. name: Karen Greenhalgh
subject: The Greenhalgh Gazette - February 24, 2023
disclaimer_text: Paid for and Authorized by Karen Greenhalgh for Delegate
body:
| | |
---
| | **Restoring Trust and Transparency in the Government**
---
| | We are finishing the final week of the legislative session in Richmond. House Republicans have been working countless hours in subcommittees, committees, and on the House floor to consider hundreds of Senate bills within the abbreviated timeline of the short session. Last week, the House and Senate passed separate budgets with key differences in funding priorities – including tax relief for Virginians’ – that must be reconciled. My colleagues and I have stressed the urgency of beginning budget negotiations with the Senate to have a final version ready for the Governor to sign, negotiations now underway. Each day that we step foot in the Capitol, we intend to pass legislation that will make a positive and lasting impact for Virginians. This mindset has enabled us to pass significant tax relief, invest in our schools, and make our communities safer. We have also made it our mission to restore trust and transparency in our elections to ensure the government runs in the best interest of all Virginians. After all, democracy cannot thrive without a electoral process people trust. Just a few years ago, the Democrat-led General Assembly passed sweeping legislation that diminished that trust by diluting our election laws in several significant ways. Over the past weeks, House Republicans advanced legislation to reverse these bad policies and replace them with pragmatic ones. We passed legislation to restore the requirement to show photo identification when voting (HB 1444, Ware). This simple measure – as commonplace as showing an ID to buy alcohol at the grocery store – is an effective deterrent to reduce voter fraud by simply authenticating their identity. Moreover, we sought to tighten absentee voting rules by repealing voter drop boxes (HB 1693, McGuire) to ensure unrestricted “vote dumps” cannot occur. House Republicans also passed bills to bring the Commonwealth back toward the original idea of "Truth in Sentencing” while increasing transparency in our day-to-day governance. In addition to key pieces of legislation that would have ensured criminals who have committed particularly heinous crimes would stay off the streets for longer periods of time, we also advanced legislation that would shine a light on the actions of the Parole Board to implement real accountability and reinforce the safety of Virginians. While the Democrat-led Senate blocked several of these measures, we remain committed to exercising good governance and preserving the sacred tenets of our democratic process. The outcome of these bills only strengthens our commitment to continue fighting to restore trust and transparency in our government. As the legislative process comes to an end in Richmond, I encourage you to track my bills and other legislation in the General Assembly at [www.lis.virginia.gov](http://sendpatriot.campstrategic.com/go/1/23a9f17af7861f4f16f79d307c796b93/37923/028199a93c59bfa3/f1fe168d72a65a0dc3d6b838967d376c/37923). If I can be of assistance to you or you would like to share your position on my legislation, please reach out to me at [DelKGreenhalgh@house.virginia.gov](mailto:DelKGreenhalgh@house.virginia.gov) or 804-698-1085.
---
| | **My Personal and Legislative Updates**
---
| |
---
**Jewish Advocacy Day** On Jewish Advocacy Day, a number of individuals from the United Jewish Federation of Tidewater came out to speak with me and express their thoughts on some of the legislation in this session, and their hopes looking forward to the future. I am grateful to work alongside Joel Bond and others from the Jewish Community Relations Board to fight antisemitism in this commonwealth.
---
| |
---
| | **Men of Faith** The Men of Faith is an incredible organization that works with African American males in the Virginia Beach public school system to aid them in reaching academic achievement and their highest academic potential. They were formed as a faith-based entity desiring to address the education disparity gap found among students in troubled or impoverished homes, especially those of African American background. I was honored to introduce them this past week in the Virginia House of Delegates.
---
---
| | **Virginia Beach Amateur Radio Club** With this bill I am fighting to protect women's rights in sports and their athletic futures by ensuring biological men compete only on men's and coed teams. After working with the Virginia Beach Amateur radio club to create HB1376 allowing their vehicles to flash amber lights while in motion in cases of emergency, I am thrilled to say the bill passed both the house and the senate unanimously. Thank you to those who came out on behalf of the group.
---
---
| | **Generation Joshua** GGeneration Joshua is an organization dedicated to engaging the next generation of youth in civics so that they can become leaders in every sphere of life. Generation Joshua has trained more than 28,000 students through online courses, immersive simulations, and in-person advocacy groups, and has been utilized by public schools, private schools, and home schools to provide students with a forum in which they can strengthen their beliefs and put them to practice. Earlier this month, to mark their 20th anniversary and my gratitude for all their service, I was honored with the ability to meet with a group representing them, and to introduce a Joint Resolution commending the group.
---
| | **Fire Service Award Congratulations**
---
| | Congratulations to Jessica Xenakis, Life Safety Education Supervisor with the Virginia Beach Fire Department for receiving the 2022 Governor’s Fire Service Award for Excellence in Virginia Community Risk Reduction!
---
| Congratulations to Barbara Morrison, public information officer in the Virginia Beach Fire Department for receiving the 2022 Governor’s Fire Service Award for Civilian Excellence in Virginia Fire Service Support!
---
| | |
---
| |
---
| Your Delegate, Karen **Delegate Karen S. Greenhalgh** **House of Delegates** 1-804-698-1085 [karengreenhalgh.com](http://sendpatriot.campstrategic.com/go/1/23a9f17af7861f4f16f79d307c796b93/37923/028199a93c59bfa3/5798ae79954e22ddc3d6b838967d376c/37923 "http://firepit.campstrategic.com/go/1/9206fe5164b4fa85041ad8cde91a9694/37923/c1e1fdff1fcd89fe/178744a2ec7ca92cc3d6b838967d376c/37923")
---
| | |
---
---
| | | [](http://sendpatriot.campstrategic.com/go/1/23a9f17af7861f4f16f79d307c796b93/37923/028199a93c59bfa3/1f073de86799d074c3d6b838967d376c/37923)| [](http://sendpatriot.campstrategic.com/go/1/23a9f17af7861f4f16f79d307c796b93/37923/028199a93c59bfa3/f3f7652d92e7d95bc3d6b838967d376c/37923)| [](http://sendpatriot.campstrategic.com/go/1/23a9f17af7861f4f16f79d307c796b93/37923/028199a93c59bfa3/89577a6dee462316c3d6b838967d376c/37923)

Peter-
Dan Crenshaw here.
This week, I highlighted a new study which quantifies and confirms what we've been saying all along: COVID lockdowns were all cost and no benefit for Democrat states. I also called out a troubling trend in school districts across the country and brought attention to the $20 trillion increase in the U.S. national debt that's projected over the next 10 years.
As always, thank you for your support.
Now, here's your brief:
---
| |
---
| | |
---
| |
---
| COVID LOCKDOWNS WERE ALL COST AND NO BENEFIT
---
| |
---
| [ ](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001mK8a1vw2ioGrqvttZvsgY7fYXsWahUrA9LZnJ6mmEfhHTazJFwS7VQCPETojOoGQynY4TsAb5H_eEgpuK8gZAZkTJurRwtPcARZl91LY7asXFf5Vl6q0W6wHyvmKm5fdccgiDX6gPv5b4PidQS7HuXS46JPF9pX-aSGBHjqEu9B7wJBVv87e3I0HNpSrBqNw7aZtzB6DjSDpzq6VstwBUQ==&c=MIGFBqx4dGD0n5a6eOQoHVKr-WMG5SwEAR5bI4zMlhz7PzG_N2TCMw==&ch=9fPbx6bKmG8Ixys6xscR9dnPmsgS7-QdU1IxpTwuch6WXPSxDNXwfw==)

| A [new study](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001mK8a1vw2ioGrqvttZvsgY7fYXsWahUrA9LZnJ6mmEfhHTazJFwS7VQCPETojOoGQynY4TsAb5H_eEgpuK8gZAZkTJurRwtPcARZl91LY7asXFf5Vl6q0W6wHyvmKm5fdccgiDX6gPv5b4PidQS7HuXS46JPF9pX-aSGBHjqEu9B7wJBVv87e3I0HNpSrBqNw7aZtzB6DjSDpzq6VstwBUQ==&c=MIGFBqx4dGD0n5a6eOQoHVKr-WMG5SwEAR5bI4zMlhz7PzG_N2TCMw==&ch=9fPbx6bKmG8Ixys6xscR9dnPmsgS7-QdU1IxpTwuch6WXPSxDNXwfw==) has quantified what we've been saying for a long time--government lockdowns were all cost, no benefit. The states that locked down during COVID suffered and so did their residents, causing people to leave for open states like Texas (which was [#2](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001mK8a1vw2ioGrqvttZvsgY7fYXsWahUrA9LZnJ6mmEfhHTazJFwS7VQCPETojOoGQVd6ORnftv4wum_aHLZwRfkr_Xk7828MVnw4gLYSC6EttcU-kEt6zld5SXGAHemhDKitiGWIkNMNFMk3d5m1SJan-CpdkiGY6y7u4IZonYGEYUxJVNOOXlQ==&c=MIGFBqx4dGD0n5a6eOQoHVKr-WMG5SwEAR5bI4zMlhz7PzG_N2TCMw==&ch=9fPbx6bKmG8Ixys6xscR9dnPmsgS7-QdU1IxpTwuch6WXPSxDNXwfw==) for domestic migration during pandemic years).
Among the findings:

* States with severe government interventions did not significantly improve health outcomes (age-adjusted and pre-existing-condition adjusted COVID mortality and all-cause excess mortality) compared to states with more restrained approaches. This may be partly because government interventions appear to have increased excess mortality from non-COVID health conditions.

* Severe government interventions were strongly correlated with worse economic (increased unemployment and decreased GDP) and educational (fewer days of in-person schooling) outcomes. The economic and education damage was most severe for lower-income families and children.

Prolonged and unnecessary restrictions like the COVID lockdowns never had the outcomes Democrats expected. In the end, they only led to increased unemployment, lower economic activity, and learning loss for lower income families. The proof is undeniable.

| A troubling new trend is emerging: AP and Honors classes are being canceled in school districts across the country. Why? Because far left school administrators say not enough minority students are enrolled in these classes, thus making them "inequitable." Instead of addressing the root of the problem by giving these disadvantaged students a better education, these administrators would rather just cancel all advanced education and pretend that puts every student on an even playing field.
Another point these school districts have conveniently ignored: erasing honors class opportunities for students in the name of "racial equity" is inherently racist. Assuming that minority students aren't capable of handling intensive, higher-level learning is a perfect example of the left's soft bigotry of low expectations. Equality means giving everyone an equal opportunity to succeed. By limiting all students' access to rigorous courses to achieve a manufactured version of equity and educational success, you're actually just ensuring that no student can live up to their full potential in the classroom.

| The Congressional Budget Office projects the U.S. national debt will increase by $20 trillion in just 10 years. This isn't sustainable, and it's also why it's perfectly reasonable for Republicans to push for savings in the budget.
Some facts for reference: only about 1/3 of our spending is discretionary, meaning the annual budget we fight about every year. This includes all spending on government agencies like Department of Defense, Homeland Security, Environmental Protect Agency, Transportation Department, etc. And the other 2/3rd? That's all "mandatory" spending, which means programs like Social Security, Medicare, and Medicaid. That's also the fastest growing area of spending because our population is aging. People are living longer and having less kids, which has made these retirement safety nets fiscally unsustainable.
Unfortunately, most people don't even know these basic facts about the budget, which makes it easy for opportunistic politicians to lie about the debt and who is to blame for the debt crisis. The truth is that it's everyone's fault, and we need everyone to be all in for a solution.

clean_body: | | | | | | | | == | Peter- Dan Crenshaw here. This week, I highlighted a new study which quantifies and confirms what we've been saying all along: COVID lockdowns were all cost and no benefit for Democrat states. I also called out a troubling trend in school districts across the country and brought attention to the $20 trillion increase in the U.S. national debt that's projected over the next 10 years. As always, thank you for your support. Now, here's your brief: | | | | | | | | COVID LOCKDOWNS WERE ALL COST AND NO BENEFIT | | | == | == | | | A == has quantified what we've been saying for a long time--government lockdowns were all cost, no benefit. The states that locked down during COVID suffered and so did their residents, causing people to leave for open states like Texas (which was == for domestic migration during pandemic years). Among the findings: States with severe government interventions did not significantly improve health outcomes (age-adjusted and pre-existing-condition adjusted COVID mortality and all-cause excess mortality) compared to states with more restrained approaches. This may be partly because government interventions appear to have increased excess mortality from non-COVID health conditions. Severe government interventions were strongly correlated with worse economic (increased unemployment and decreased GDP) and educational (fewer days of in-person schooling) outcomes. The economic and education damage was most severe for lower-income families and children. Prolonged and unnecessary restrictions like the COVID lockdowns never had the outcomes Democrats expected. In the end, they only led to increased unemployment, lower economic activity, and learning loss for lower income families. The proof is undeniable. | | | | | | | | THE SOFT BIGOTRY OF LOW EXPECTATIONS | | | == | == | | | A troubling new trend is emerging: AP and Honors classes are being canceled in school districts across the country. Why? Because far left school administrators say not enough minority students are enrolled in these classes, thus making them "inequitable." Instead of addressing the root of the problem by giving these disadvantaged students a better education, these administrators would rather just cancel all advanced education and pretend that puts every student on an even playing field. Another point these school districts have conveniently ignored: erasing honors class opportunities for students in the name of "racial equity" is inherently racist. Assuming that minority students aren't capable of handling intensive, higher-level learning is a perfect example of the left's soft bigotry of low expectations. Equality means giving everyone an equal opportunity to succeed. By limiting all students' access to rigorous courses to achieve a manufactured version of equity and educational success, you're actually just ensuring that no student can live up to their full potential in the classroom. | | | | | | | | A FISCAL DISASTER IS LOOMING | == | == | The Congressional Budget Office projects the U.S. national debt will increase by $20 trillion in just 10 years. This isn't sustainable, and it's also why it's perfectly reasonable for Republicans to push for savings in the budget. Some facts for reference: only about 1/3 of our spending is discretionary, meaning the annual budget we fight about every year. This includes all spending on government agencies like Department of Defense, Homeland Security, Environmental Protect Agency, Transportation Department, etc. And the other 2/3rd? That's all "mandatory" spending, which means programs like Social Security, Medicare, and Medicaid. That's also the fastest growing area of spending because our population is aging. People are living longer and having less kids, which has made these retirement safety nets fiscally unsustainable. Unfortunately, most people don't even know these basic facts about the budget, which makes it easy for opportunistic politicians to lie about the debt and who is to blame for the debt crisis. The truth is that it's everyone's fault, and we need everyone to be all in for a solution. | | | | | | | | == | | | == | | | | | | | | QUOTE OF THE WEEK | | "The only way that has ever been discovered to have a lot of people cooperate together voluntarily is through the free market. And that's why it's so essential to preserving individual freedom." -Milton Friedman | | | | | | | | ON SOCIAL MEDIA | | | == | | | == | | | == | | | | | | | | | | WEEKEND READS National Post: == Shellenberger: == The Free Press: == | That's it for this week's campaign brief. Thanks for reading. That's it for this week's campaign brief. If you like what you read and want to support my campaign to grow the conservative movement, ==. name: Elizabeth Warren
subject: re: Ticketmaster
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warren Democrats
body:
I’m tired of greedy corporations gobbling up power — and I’m fighting back. | | | [ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59204484/393559425/-1964596891?ms=em-header&refcode=EMZ11X_COR_E_20230224_V1S3_rem2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDgvMS84NzkzMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3MGNjODMxYS05MmI0LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=nTqWSkpHVaIW4mPTKhcIPmAwq4gvqUPsWC2ILwAkXTI=&emci=57c15983-91b4-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&emdi=70cc831a-92b4-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&ceid=9027908)
---
Have you had a hard time buying concert tickets lately? Has someone you know been struggling trying to see their favorite artist? From Taylor Swift to Bad Bunny to Beyoncé and beyond, when musicians go on tour, their fans usually have to go through one company. A company that keeps botching the process and making people pay through the nose. A company called Ticketmaster. Now, you know me — when I see a story like this, I think about concentrated corporate power. So do you mind if I nerd out for a minute? For decades now, the federal government has taken a hands-off approach to antitrust enforcement. Giant corporations have been allowed to buy out their competitors. They’ve been able to gain monopoly or near-monopoly power. And because they’re just about the only game in town, they’ve been able to hurt workers with lower wages and hurt consumers with higher prices, worse service, or both. Exhibit A: In 2010, the government let Live Nation, a huge concert-promoting company, buy Ticketmaster, a huge concert-ticket-selling company. Together, they became a one-two punch of market dominance. And they’ve been pummeling fans, artists, and venues in the jaw ever since. But here’s the thing: The federal government has the power to re-examine mergers like Live Nation’s purchase of Ticketmaster, break them up, and restore competition in the marketplace. [_**With high prices and terrible service, Ticketmaster shows the danger of corporate concentration. I’ve been calling to break up big monopolies, and the Biden-Harris administration is taking action, but we need to fight side by side as a grassroots movement to defeat powerful corporate interests. Add your name to say you’re in this fight.**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59204485/393559426/-871118716?refcode=EMZ11X_COR_E_20230224_V1S3_rem2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDgvMS84NzkzMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3MGNjODMxYS05MmI0LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=nTqWSkpHVaIW4mPTKhcIPmAwq4gvqUPsWC2ILwAkXTI=&emci=57c15983-91b4-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&emdi=70cc831a-92b4-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&ceid=9027908) | [**ADD YOUR NAME**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59204486/393559427/-871118716?refcode=EMZ11X_COR_E_20230224_V1S3_rem2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDgvMS84NzkzMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3MGNjODMxYS05MmI0LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=nTqWSkpHVaIW4mPTKhcIPmAwq4gvqUPsWC2ILwAkXTI=&emci=57c15983-91b4-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&emdi=70cc831a-92b4-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&ceid=9027908)
---
Of course, corporate concentration goes way beyond concert tickets — it’s infested our entire economy. Big agribusinesses are crushing family farmers and jacking up prices for consumers. Big tech companies are elbowing out startups and using our private information for profit. Big airlines are getting away with higher fares for lower-quality service.
Pure and simple, the American people are suffering under the thumb of corporations that are just too big and too powerful.
I’ll keep calling on the administration to use all of its tools to fight back against corporate monopolies. I’ve also got a bill — the Prohibiting Anticompetitive Mergers Act — to ban the biggest, most competition-crushing mergers, restore competition, and bring down prices for consumers.
**I’ll stay in the fight — but it’s only possible because I’ve got people like you fighting alongside me.**
[_**Add your name if you agree: It’s time to take on big corporate monopolies and level the playing field so they can’t get away with ridiculously high prices and ridiculously crummy service.**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59204487/393559428/-871118716?refcode=EMZ11X_COR_E_20230224_V1S3_rem2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDgvMS84NzkzMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3MGNjODMxYS05MmI0LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=nTqWSkpHVaIW4mPTKhcIPmAwq4gvqUPsWC2ILwAkXTI=&emci=57c15983-91b4-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&emdi=70cc831a-92b4-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&ceid=9027908)

clean_body: I’m tired of greedy corporations gobbling up power — and I’m fighting back. | | | Have you had a hard time buying concert tickets lately? Has someone you know been struggling trying to see their favorite artist? From Taylor Swift to Bad Bunny to Beyoncé and beyond, when musicians go on tour, their fans usually have to go through one company. A company that keeps botching the process and making people pay through the nose. A company called Ticketmaster. Now, you know me — when I see a story like this, I think about concentrated corporate power. So do you mind if I nerd out for a minute? For decades now, the federal government has taken a hands-off approach to antitrust enforcement. Giant corporations have been allowed to buy out their competitors. They’ve been able to gain monopoly or near-monopoly power. And because they’re just about the only game in town, they’ve been able to hurt workers with lower wages and hurt consumers with higher prices, worse service, or both. Exhibit A: In 2010, the government let Live Nation, a huge concert-promoting company, buy Ticketmaster, a huge concert-ticket-selling company. Together, they became a one-two punch of market dominance. And they’ve been pummeling fans, artists, and venues in the jaw ever since. But here’s the thing: The federal government has the power to re-examine mergers like Live Nation’s purchase of Ticketmaster, break them up, and restore competition in the marketplace. | Of course, corporate concentration goes way beyond concert tickets — it’s infested our entire economy. Big agribusinesses are crushing family farmers and jacking up prices for consumers. Big tech companies are elbowing out startups and using our private information for profit. Big airlines are getting away with higher fares for lower-quality service. Pure and simple, the American people are suffering under the thumb of corporations that are just too big and too powerful. I’ll keep calling on the administration to use all of its tools to fight back against corporate monopolies. I’ve also got a bill — the Prohibiting Anticompetitive Mergers Act — to ban the biggest, most competition-crushing mergers, restore competition, and bring down prices for consumers. I’ll stay in the fight — but it’s only possible because I’ve got people like you fighting alongside me. Thanks for being a part of this, Elizabeth = | | | | | | ---|---|---|--- | Paid for by Warren Democrats All content © 2023 Warren Democrats, All Rights Reserved PO Box 171375, Boston, MA 02117

Hi Peter,

I’m Col. Pam Stevenson, and I’m fighting to be the People’s Lawyer as Kentucky’s next Attorney General.

**_I’m no stranger to defying expectations, Peter._** When I entered the U.S. Air Force, I knew I wanted to serve by teaching justice and human rights around the world. Despite a lack of women in leadership in the U.S. military ranks, I served more than 27 years as a J.A.G. officer.
[](https://an.collectivepac.org/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953x7ESlwpd39wyaaM2DOMklJogtuIGl7T0PFlC2l_3jjzu0Bh68iAtN8HJxbIbAu26Mnriz2JJHaR_ZVD4AMCFqPA7h9tnSFjDVIoEMwXa0U0pPIEpygxq7LZnoIL1mj6schn8Ujh2U1-qbeq0sDWpflDt9R7x2-S3jKnazFCcdKn2YYiqC9eBs95shZinEdyxnSHI9b9_cZarrUpE2gLLKrC0zGM0IDC6SDId4fLNr-8TYcD3SJP8ip3PVszYO6yV_NoDN6iA9VxOD0xzgwDErrHXzH6E-5STFBSPOcaRElnykfFNTBwQ7RqfPcB775w6VzO-TwB2GrLc06pHWuNn-P7ATgODl5gQqbIu0fMPP0Jvr9CzmLZ_OwIh484R6og3TGbbZ2UDf74fgv_oZaFIoppx1RuFtKraHHhrcDv5dXYvTWCqtuWeMzblidTDe-R1Hg1dhuPhtrdBMqTnJ-s3et9KrQ-G6yFHmI15AzuwJdgoyUElM_td9AVo-vF8DplWY/3tz/rc46jVIFQlG7H55ftiQpHg/h2/tXpwxiHygfnb7ZIEik498lifgkpCOHs-zgCTre_osck)
When I returned home from my work fighting for justice and human rights abroad, I continued the fight right here in Kentucky. **I’m just one of four Black women ever elected to the Kentucky state legislature.** I’m the first Black woman to secure a major party's nomination for KY Attorney General. And at this very moment, I’m fighting to officially recognize the last day of February, the day connecting Black and Women’s History Months, as a day to honor Black women.

Growing up in West Louisville around the church my grandfather founded and my time in the Air Force taught me that with hard work, grit, and dedication to service for others, anything is possible. I've always fought to ensure our families are cared for, our communities are safe, and our children are protected. **_But to win this race, I need your help._**

[So, Peter, will you join the fight to break historical barriers and ensure every Kentuckian can not only survive but thrive by splitting $10 or whatever you can afford between The Collective PAC and Col. clean_body: My race for AG is a historic moment for Kentucky. | | If you want to help The Collective PAC build Black political power up and down the ballot, and across the country, then you’re in the right place. If you'd like to unsubscribe | | Hi Peter, I’m Col. Pam Stevenson, and I’m fighting to be the People’s Lawyer as Kentucky’s next Attorney General. I’m no stranger to defying expectations, Peter. When I entered the U.S. Air Force, I knew I wanted to serve by teaching justice and human rights around the world. Despite a lack of women in leadership in the U.S. military ranks, I served more than 27 years as a J.A.G. officer. When I returned home from my work fighting for justice and human rights abroad, I continued the fight right here in Kentucky. I’m just one of four Black women ever elected to the Kentucky state legislature. I’m the first Black woman to secure a major party's nomination for KY Attorney General. And at this very moment, I’m fighting to officially recognize the last day of February, the day connecting Black and Women’s History Months, as a day to honor Black women. Growing up in West Louisville around the church my grandfather founded and my time in the Air Force taught me that with hard work, grit, and dedication to service for others, anything is possible. I've always fought to ensure our families are cared for, our communities are safe, and our children are protected. But to win this race, I need your help. | If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will be processed immediately and split between Col. Pam Stevenson and The Collective PAC. | | | | | | Together, we can finally send the People’s Lawyer to the Kentucky Attorney General’s office. Onward, –Col. Pam The Collective PAC is strategically focused on creating a truly equitable democracy where our nation’s local, state and federal governments have diverse and talented elected leadership and Black people are fully represented in positions of power to create the policies necessary to progress our communities forward. Since our inception in 2016, we have helped 110 candidates win general elections at the local, state and federal levels. We strive to support the next wave of progressive and unapologetic Black candidates who will usher in a sea of change in our political system while helping America fulfill its promise of democracy by creating a government that will truly be for the people and of the people. We’ll keep this short: Democrats have a hill to climb next election season. We’re fighting Republicans at every turn — state, local, and federal races. We’re in danger of losing progress on issues like abortion rights, job creation, voting rights, and affordable health care. clean_body: We have to raise just $1500 more online to hit our February goal. We can blow that goal out of the water. | | | Hi Peter, It might be a shorter month, but you’re not too late to help. We’ll keep this short: Democrats have a hill to climb next election season. We’re fighting Republicans at every turn — state, local, and federal races. The next elections will have huge implications for policy across the country. We’re in danger of losing progress on issues like abortion rights, job creation, voting rights, and affordable health care. In states like Wisconsin where one seat can make all the difference in protecting reproductive rights and democracy, it's paramount that Democrats outperform and outraise our opponents. United Rural Democrats is putting in the work now to make sure we have a chance to expand our progress in 2023 and 2024. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your secure donation will go through immediately: Thank you for your contribution to Democrats. – United Rural Democrats United Rural Democrats (URD) is leading the effort to rebuild the Democratic Party in rural America. If we're going to take back our country, we need a 50-state strategy to organize in every community across the country. Will you support our network of activists on the ground as we work to rebuild our party's infrastructure and flip these areas blue?

name: Team Dolan
subject: ICYMI: Matt Dolan Statement on the One-Year Anniversary of Vladimir Putin’s Invasion of Ukraine
body:
| | | | [](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001phlUwFZ4st5a3aS4uTGe53MXqydaUVeDpmbvneg1eBjx1N5VDWIDSQF-yJrnHY8Qe_ZEL4Uqm7LDiNRRCrEs0d7eEAvJfGcfF8gemct_Yd0IWNTEYaMz7n7DbyrEv2GAXaFx8nOS2oio7t7Kz7cH_g==&c=4jgMpJkLqjOn81_C9QRwZJAYAAE_Y9Fkt-izWOkkO5zbBfqmKfxrAg==&ch=DGTh3D7Guwu9a0kE30_ZMkn4hyXdzdu4SV2c1HRATMLi5va1TOKSHA==)
---
| For Immediate Release: February 24 2023 | [press@DolanForOhio.com](mailto:press@DolanForOhio.com)

Matt Dolan Statement on the One-Year Anniversary of Vladimir Putin's Invasion of Ukraine

Cleveland, OH: U.S. Senate candidate Matt Dolan released the following statement on the one-year anniversary of Vladimir Putin's invasion of Ukraine. "This week marks the one-year anniversary of Vladimir Putin's unprovoked invasion in Ukraine. Every day since, the free world has been reminded of the powerful lessons that freedom is fragile and weakness invites aggression. "To date, the United States has given nearly $50 billion in aid to Ukraine; a figure equating to approximately .02% of our national GDP. These resources have played a pivotal role in Ukraine's defense and the counteroffensive against Russia. Congressional oversight and accountability must go hand in hand with how Americans' tax dollars continue to be spent. The Russian invasion is a threat to global security that requires collective defense and burden sharing between America and her allies. That's exactly why other nations, particularly other NATO members, should be called upon to provide a greater degree of military, financial and humanitarian support to the Ukrainian people. "One year on, it's more clear than ever that defending freedom abroad requires American leadership in the world and embracing hard truths at home. The Biden Administration's policies continue to give America's adversaries reason to question our resolve. From delayed implementation of economic sanctions, to dithering on providing Ukraine with military equipment, and reducing American energy production at home, missteps continue to plague Administration's response on the world stage. "The United States does not have an active military presence in Ukraine. That should certainly remain the case. But let there be no mistake, stopping Russia in Ukraine is in vital interest to America's national security. Vladimir Putin is hellbent on delegitimizing NATO and reversing the causes of freedom and self-determination established by Western democracy. Allowing Russia to establish a sustained foothold inside Ukraine would effectively forfeit the nation's strategic industry and agriculture, impacting international food and energy prices, and even exacerbating the migrant crisis throughout the West. It would also invite a larger war across Europe that would drag in U.S. forces and ruin our deterrence in Asia and the Middle East. "A withdrawal of U.S. support from Ukraine would also embolden rogue regimes in China and Iran to further threaten Taiwan or even Israel. The United States, working in close concert with our allies, has little choice but to more effectively provide military, economic and diplomatic support for Kyiv to safeguard Ukraine's sovereignty and send a message to authoritarian regimes that sovereign states cannot be invaded and subjected to slaughter with impunity. "May God continue to be with the free people of Ukraine and their family members here in Ohio and across the United States."
For more information on Matt Dolan's campaign for U.S. Senate, email [press@dolanforohio.com.](mailto:press@dolanforohio.com.)
###

| Follow Matt on Social Media!

name: Fairness Project
subject: TELL CONGRESS: Extend the Child Tax Credit >>
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Fairness Project The Fairness Project
body:
Extending the Child Tax Credit would cut childhood poverty in half practically overnight. | [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/59190704/393514086/765663662?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRlAvMS84NTQ3OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmZjQ4OWJmYi02OWI0LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=yP6U_gYRGkkd0da0SNK1DHQCPGIxBuw6IvhZib1tqzA=&emci=76654981-d9b2-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&emdi=ff489bfb-69b4-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&ceid=561582) | Friends, Earlier this month during his State of the Union address, President Biden called for Congress to pass the Child Tax Credit again — a move that would bring financial relief to millions of America’s working families. A new Congress means _**now**_ is the time to put pressure on Congress to protect the hardworking Americans they were just elected to represent. It’s past time that Congress made a commitment to prioritizing working people in America and their families by ensuring benefits like the Child Tax Credit **_and_** paid family leave. [**If you agree, will you sign on to our petition and demand the 118th Congress re-extend the Child Tax clean_body: Extending the Child Tax Credit would cut childhood poverty in half practically overnight. | | Friends, Earlier this month during his State of the Union address, President Biden called for Congress to pass the Child Tax Credit again — a move that would bring financial relief to millions of America’s working families. A new Congress means now is the time to put pressure on Congress to protect the hardworking Americans they were just elected to represent. It’s past time that Congress made a commitment to prioritizing working people in America and their families by ensuring benefits like the Child Tax Credit and paid family leave. At the Fairness Project, we’ve been using ballot measures to pass policies for families that provide real economic support to their communities. Extending the Child Tax Credit would cut childhood poverty in half practically overnight. Thanks, friends. — Fairness Project Thanks to grassroots supporters like you, our team at the Fairness Project has won 31 people-powered ballot measure campaigns since 2016, changing over 18 million lives by expanding health care, raising wages, guaranteeing paid leave, and ending predatory lending directly at the ballot box when politicians refuse to act. Your support allows us to make this progress possible.** = = Contributions or gifts to the Fairness Project are not deductible as charitable contributions for federal income tax purposes. | | Local government has never been more important.
[](https://click.everyaction.com/k/59190749/393514301/-258323618?sourceid=1048674&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzODRiNTIwMi02YWI0LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=D0ttGz0XO9stP1u68YgnzXzpIZTjEgmRWP_uu1qKXmk=&emci=04499bfb-69b4-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&emdi=384b5202-6ab4-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&ceid=5177934) Mayors are essential to our communities, Peter.

Their leadership and advocacy are crucial to building strong, vibrant cities and defending our rights — especially today, with _Roe v. Wade_ overturned and voting rights under attack.

Every city in America deserves a Democratic pro-choice mayor, and this year, we're starting with four: Jacksonville, Florida; Chicago, Illinois; Madison, Wisconsin; and Lincoln, Nebraska. [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/59190750/393514302/-258323618?sourceid=1048674&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzODRiNTIwMi02YWI0LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=D0ttGz0XO9stP1u68YgnzXzpIZTjEgmRWP_uu1qKXmk=&emci=04499bfb-69b4-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&emdi=384b5202-6ab4-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&ceid=5177934) At a time when extremism runs rampant and many elected officials are actively working to pull back our rights, local government has never been more important. That's why we're proud to endorse four fierce pro-choice champions in mayoral races across the country.
* For far too long the residents of Jacksonville, Florida, have been led by extremist politicians. **Donna Deegan** is looking to change that. As mayor, she will be committed to championing improved infrastructure, affordable health care, and a thriving economy for all.
* As one of the country's few African American mayors in major U.S. cities, Mayor **Lori Lightfoot** has fought tirelessly to improve the lives of the people of Chicago, Illinois, and protect reproductive freedom — _and her election is less than a week away_!
* **Satya Rhodes-Conway** has been an effective and compassionate mayor, serving the people of Madison, Wisconsin, with dedication and tenacity. Her commitment to democracy and equal rights for all will ensure that she will accomplish even greater things for Wisconsinites in her next term.
* **Leirion Gaylor Baird** has been a faithful and fearless mayor for the city of Lincoln, Nebraska, working every day to protect democracy and improve the lives of her constituents. EMILYs List is proud to stand by her campaign for reelection.

****We know these women can make a huge impact in their communities, but first, they need to WIN. Donate now to help elect women like Donna, Lori, Satya, and Leirion in 2023:****

****[https://emilyslist.org/go/Elect-Women-This-February](https://click.everyaction.com/k/59190757/393514309/-258323618?sourceid=1048674&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzODRiNTIwMi02YWI0LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=D0ttGz0XO9stP1u68YgnzXzpIZTjEgmRWP_uu1qKXmk=&emci=04499bfb-69b4-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&emdi=384b5202-6ab4-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&ceid=5177934)**** Thank you,

the EMILYs List Campaigns team

name: Team Foushee
subject: the Republican party is off the rails
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Foushee for Congress

body:
\- - - | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/ec6a6eef4cd7/lori-chavez-deremer-announces-candidacy-for-congress-8117391?e=c87c5d8d97)
---
| | [](https://gobigmediainc.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a2447b89afe54066435f04744&id=a359cd7592&e=c87c5d8d97)
---
| Peter,

_**Oregon is facing economic challenges that have taken a toll on our businesses and communities, leaving many of us struggling to make ends meet. That's why Lori is determined to restore our economy and create a brighter future for our families and communities.**_

As Vice Chairwoman of the Economic Development, Public Buildings, and Emergency Management Subcommittee, Lori has the opportunity to make a real impact. With her leadership and expertise, she is working tirelessly to **bring jobs back to Oregon, support small businesses, and help our communities recover.**

[_**But she needs your support Peter. By contributing $100, $75, or even $25, you can help Lori continue her work to bring investment to our state, create jobs, and support small businesses. >>>**_](https://gobigmediainc.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a2447b89afe54066435f04744&id=67d8da0e0b&e=c87c5d8d97)

Oregon for Lori

I can’t do it without patriots like you, so Thank you for your support, Senator Rick Scott | | Paid for by Rick Scott for Florida | | Contributions to this committee are not deductible as charitable contributions for Federal income tax purposes. **Josh has always said - no one is above the law and if Donald Trump broke it, he should be held accountable.**
Now we wait for a decision from the Fulton County DA on whether she is going to charge Trump. In the meantime, we’re going to keep fighting to make sure the public understands why Trump can _never_ become President ever again.
Crazy times!
\- Team Harder

The LA Times just released a brand-new poll of the Democratic contenders to be California’s next U.S. Senator, and**Katie is trailing the leader by just two points.**
Adam Schiff: 22%

name: Team Brown
subject: We can’t wait any longer
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Shontel Brown for Congress
body:
No more families need to suffer.
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59209640/393563063/1971969537?refcode=EM_PET_230224PoliceReform_PoliceReform_TEAM_90DO_5_NG_TESTB_logo&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84Nzg0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwZmZhNDZlZS05NmI0LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=G1wwWR2URawBnASIdcb5wM5k3x8oOmfQ_56CuWovE3Y=&emci=a9bd8e0a-f9b2-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&emdi=0ffa46ee-96b4-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&ceid=23746811) Peter,

The state of Ohio has a history of police brutality, including the tragic murders of Tamir Rice, Donovan Lewis, and Jayland Walker, among countless others. The epidemic of police violence in Ohio has long been out of control, and our demands for accountability have prompted the Justice Department to act.

After a two-year review into the Columbus police, the Justice Department found a need for longer training and improvements in hiring and accountability. Black and Brown communities have long known this, but now we have conclusive evidence that urgent reform is needed.

**Representative Shontel Brown has been fighting for police reform in Congress and working to ensure our communities are properly protected and served.** At President Biden’s State of the Union, she proudly invited and stood with Samaria Rice and the families of victims of police violence to send a message that their voices won’t be silenced.

These families and so many others deserve the community-based solutions to police violence that they’ve been promised, and we need you to speak up and stand with them.

[Now is the time to take action, Peter. We must call on Congress to pass sweeping reform as soon as possible. Will you sign our petition demanding Congress act on police reform now? ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59209649/393563064/1971969537?refcode=EM_PET_230224PoliceReform_PoliceReform_TEAM_90DO_5_NG_TESTB_l1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84Nzg0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwZmZhNDZlZS05NmI0LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=G1wwWR2URawBnASIdcb5wM5k3x8oOmfQ_56CuWovE3Y=&emci=a9bd8e0a-f9b2-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&emdi=0ffa46ee-96b4-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&ceid=23746811) [ADD YOUR NAME](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59209651/393563067/1971969537?refcode=EM_PET_230224PoliceReform_PoliceReform_TEAM_90DO_5_NG_TESTB_b1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84Nzg0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwZmZhNDZlZS05NmI0LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=G1wwWR2URawBnASIdcb5wM5k3x8oOmfQ_56CuWovE3Y=&emci=a9bd8e0a-f9b2-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&emdi=0ffa46ee-96b4-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&ceid=23746811) I know we can count on you,

Team Brown | As a Cuyahoga County Council member and Chairwoman of the Cuyahoga County Democratic Party, Shontel Brown has spent the last decade serving the people of Ohio and building coalitions. Now, as the Congresswoman for Ohio’s 11th District, she will fight for affordable quality health care, jobs that pay fair wages, a strong public education system, and equity and justice for all. **Will you join her and be the change we need?**

name: PA Democrats
subject: A spotlight on Lieutenant Governor Austin Davis
disclaimer_text: Paid for by The Pennsylvania Democratic Party
body:
This week, we want to highlight the newly elected Lieutenant Governor Austin Davis. | | |
---
| | Dear Peter, As we continue to share the achievements of Black leaders in Pennsylvania throughout the course of Black History Month, this week, we want to highlight the newly elected Lieutenant Governor Austin Davis. **In November 2022, Lt. Governor Davis made history when he was elected as the first Black Lieutenant Governor of Pennsylvania and the youngest Lieutenant Governor elected in our nation’s history.** **But this wasn’t the first time Lt. Governor Davis has made history.** In 2018, Davis successfully ran for the Pennsylvania House of Representatives, becoming the first African-American to serve as a state representative for Allegheny County's 35th Legislative District.
---
---
Growing up in the Mon Valley of Western Pennsylvania, Lt. Governor Davis saw the struggles of working families firsthand and knew early on that he wanted to dedicate his life and career to public service. Throughout his career, Lt. Governor Davis has fought for economic equality, worked to lift people out of poverty, secured fair funding for education, and worked to improve the lives of the people of Western Pennsylvania. We are grateful for Lt. Governor Davis' leadership and look forward to seeing him work alongside Governor Shapiro to move Pennsylvania forward. Keep up with Lt. clean_body: This week, we want to highlight the newly elected Lieutenant Governor Austin Davis. | | | | | Dear Peter, As we continue to share the achievements of Black leaders in Pennsylvania throughout the course of Black History Month, this week, we want to highlight the newly elected Lieutenant Governor Austin Davis. In November 2022, Lt. Governor Davis made history when he was elected as the first Black Lieutenant Governor of Pennsylvania and the youngest Lieutenant Governor elected in our nation’s history. But this wasn’t the first time Lt. Governor Davis has made history. In 2018, Davis successfully ran for the Pennsylvania House of Representatives, becoming the first African-American to serve as a state representative for Allegheny County's 35th Legislative District. Growing up in the Mon Valley of Western Pennsylvania, Lt. Governor Davis saw the struggles of working families firsthand and knew early on that he wanted to dedicate his life and career to public service. Throughout his career, Lt. Governor Davis has fought for economic equality, worked to lift people out of poverty, secured fair funding for education, and worked to improve the lives of the people of Western Pennsylvania. We are grateful for Lt. Governor Davis' leadership and look forward to seeing him work alongside Governor Shapiro to move Pennsylvania forward. Keep up with Lt. Governor Davis' work by following him on , or . Thank you, Pennsylvania Democrats | Thank you for supporting the Pennsylvania Democratic Party. Pennsylvania is one of the most important swing states in the country. Donald Trump won Pennsylvania in 2016, and in 2020, we flipped our Commonwealth blue and elected Joe Biden. If we're going to elect Democrats up and down the ballot in 2022, we need your help. = Emails are an important way for us to stay in touch with our supporters.
