name: Brent Bozell
subject: Using a tragedy to sow division
body:
This is how deprived The New York Times has become.

[](https://links.freedomnationtoday.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=58725bf74d904ad2bb012f3734baf7a3&_e=cDAZ2uqn4uFNMUux5HjRQ0pwMUGpin7V70r1HQRcg3A7EtBpx27GKTgcx2LTK5d4Dke8yLI-U4X5W0hh90IhGP-w3PbQJg10HTyU6Z_W7B1TfW2jmBlGN_1f5S_uAAmskWhoRZPbQzxunjnq-ekqwCk9B-Qn1XviTyBW6Sd8lBgZgobYNfnsnsI0wswWeWi1DSV84sFXM4jLfPPi-FqnwWMnntgfrP9tFRLeMYW6GGx4pP2meXauzsepWbymRxTnyrt7XiqCFgn5Mi-8EkHefQ%3D%3D)

Friend,

This is how deprived The New York Times has become.

[](https://links.freedomnationtoday.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=58725bf74d904ad2bb012f3734baf7a3&_e=cDAZ2uqn4uFNMUux5HjRQ0pwMUGpin7V70r1HQRcg3A7EtBpx27GKTgcx2LTK5d4Dke8yLI-U4X5W0hh90IhGP-w3PbQJg10HTyU6Z_W7B1TfW2jmBlGN_1f5S_uAAms3l7bstbf8nneqcZf0KmyhO5P8AhbcoW1is0IF7jk-Ja1BDPLX_zRT_gQVQDpbKAKZa3alqp54WRH3QTf7-VjXdPdxiINy5fZqVKPknU-sXZw3aj6z6QthWDMOX9uDuEpmvgi_4imtmqurz_UdaLutA%3D%3D)
Instead of caring for the families in Ohio affected by the chemical spill, The New York Times is attacking conservatives who disagree with Biden.

They hate you more than they care about people in Ohio.

They would rather ban your way of life by taking away gas stoves than report on Biden's atrocities.

Let me introduce myself. My name is Brent Bozell, and I'm the Founder and President of the Media Research Center (MRC).

**MRC is the only conservative organization dedicated to exposing the radical left-wing agenda in the media and holding them accountable for their corruption and lies.**

Our goal is to raise $10,000 this week to expose The New York Times and other corrupt media organizations.

[_**Friend, will you send $10, $35, $50, or any amount to expose the true evil in media? Click here to send your support >>>**_](https://links.freedomnationtoday.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=58725bf74d904ad2bb012f3734baf7a3&_e=cDAZ2uqn4uFNMUux5HjRQ0pwMUGpin7V70r1HQRcg3A7EtBpx27GKTgcx2LTK5d4Dke8yLI-U4X5W0hh90IhGP-w3PbQJg10HTyU6Z_W7B1TfW2jmBlGN_1f5S_uAAmsr7SkSaN0eK-D5NfeNWUuKHG_4TpYqFtZTFvsy1B9V7JlidtQZm4wrRJQVw21chSo5acII-afhawGqDoZBEW6IIONdcU7dHUKpFq5n1KMDL6PhhrOsapEm-7zI9rapGRHsGKrBS_f3xRlHKQxHfk3fQ%3D%3D)

The New York Times will say anything that goes against Biden is _" right-wing,"_ even caring for your fellow Americans.

People have every right to be critical of Biden's poor handling of the situation. But The New York Times is attempting to change the narrative and make the people concerned about it out to be the bad guys.

**The left-wing media lies to protect politicians ... and MRC exposes them.**

On Tuesday, NewsBusters reported that the broadcast networks of ABC, CBS, and NBC had abandoned the environmental fallout from the February 3 train derailment in East Palestine, Ohio.

On their flagship morning and evening shows, ABC covered it for 0 seconds, and CBS and NBC only covered it for 1 minute and 42 seconds after an evacuation order was lifted on February 8.

But since our study's publication and public outcry about the threats posed to the community's air, food, residents, and water supply, the liberal networks rediscovered the issue Wednesday morning.

They covered the story for a combined 6 minutes and 42 seconds.
* Tuesday's CBS _Evening News_ had a 31-second brief.
* CBS Mornings stayed on the case with a 2 minute and 29 second report.
* ABC's _Good Morning America_ made a similar return with 2 minutes and 16 seconds and _never made clear they had ignored it since February 8_.
* NBC's _Today_ scrapped together 2 minutes and 16 seconds to insist it still cares about East Palestine.

MRC is keeping the pressure on the media to keep them from covering up Biden's failures.

[_**Your generous support helps MRC show Americans the truth about the media. Click here to send your gift today >>>**_](https://links.freedomnationtoday.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=58725bf74d904ad2bb012f3734baf7a3&_e=cDAZ2uqn4uFNMUux5HjRQ0pwMUGpin7V70r1HQRcg3A7EtBpx27GKTgcx2LTK5d4Dke8yLI-U4X5W0hh90IhGP-w3PbQJg10HTyU6Z_W7B1TfW2jmBlGN_1f5S_uAAmsTXsyyFpO4ed986eKM1KjA3z_d8odi5OBXgs_BBIVlZ5QjJ3NGif8F0vnls3Gg9QhpaSQb-pcj_vFh3rwqfVTvUfDWgCJe6L1b3yKHcCYiN9jC-OWWsPXbcRWCOjVaEfRxJEtkPlnuFQcmP2j5IECVQ%3D%3D)

Media Research Center found the best way to expose the left-wing media.

1. MRC monitors and clips videos of all news content from major networks, social media accounts, and cable outlets.

We have over 890,000 hours of video stored on secure servers covering every nightly broadcast and more going back to 1987.

2. My team and I analyze this content and identify fake news stories, lies, and left-wing media bias.

3. **MRC 's team exposes these lies by forcing the media to report the story, sharing their reports on social media, and telling the truth.**

The Media Research Center also owns some of the largest social media pages in the conservative movement so that they can share these stories with supporters.

**When the MRC was founded in 1987, over 75% of Americans trusted the left-wing media ... now, _over 90% of Americans DO NOT trust the Fake News media_ anymore. **

But we can't do this alone, Friend. Can I count on you to join the fight for the truth? **Click below to donate. **

[_**Donate $25 >>>**_](https://links.freedomnationtoday.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=58725bf74d904ad2bb012f3734baf7a3&_e=cDAZ2uqn4uFNMUux5HjRQ0pwMUGpin7V70r1HQRcg3A7EtBpx27GKTgcx2LTK5d4Dke8yLI-U4X5W0hh90IhGP-w3PbQJg10HTyU6Z_W7B1yAVD4D5TM3j6ht-FeQrfqfrNYNsueEGniZOIFEIuV4nV6pvjiOHqrcWTYGxqyrkEfID76oNmGmtzLf18tt7-IdwTEdDfR6FR7bXFaPGszJlPp8JVv5jv8fEZmndZj5EBgtF-Lu5O9f8psMxsvtJ92BkBbE_SMoOjUFYcDMk0Ptbb_AxXkvczyJae2oIZl8Hg%3D)

subject: 2x Impact: Help us meet our critical February Deadline
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Washington State Republican Party
body:
to fund and bring momentum to our 2023/24 programs

http://wsrp.org
Ballots Are Out, Vote By November 2nd!

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=WeGlgXqOx-Y

With ballots out, now is the perfect time to get out there and volunteer on a conservative campaign. Every door you knock on in these local races matter a lot and the results of this election year will be used as a bellwether for the 2022 midterms.

We are also hosting another WSRP Statewide Day Of Action on Saturday, October 30th! Bring a friend or neighbor to volunteer for a few hours to help local conservatives win in November! Sign up here to join our Statewide Day Of Action! (https://form.jotform.com/212737084845058)
State Sen. Gildon On Vaccine Mandates
https://soundcloud.com/user-450836972/wagop-report-state-sen-chris-gildon-on-vaccine-mandates-and-democrat-anti-policing-laws
State Senator Chris Gildon (R - Puyallup) joins the podcast this week to discuss vaccine mandates, anti-policing laws, the Long-Term Care Tax, and how he got his start in politics. WAGOP is now on Apple Podcasts and Spotify, follow us and never miss an episode! Listen here. (https://soundcloud.com/user-450836972/wagop-report-state-sen-chris-gildon-on-vaccine-mandates-and-democrat-anti-policing-laws)
Republican Leaders Fight Inslee's Mandate
Thanks to our Republican leaders in the State House and Senate for fighting back and offering a strong rebuke of Inslee's harmful vaccine mandate. Below is an excerpt of their most recent statement to the press:

“We are already experiencing higher prices and empty shelves partially as a result of COVID's shutdowns and workforce shortages. Today's deadline, with its unnecessarily hardline consequences, will only make that worse when hundreds, if not thousands, lose their jobs and are denied unemployment benefits. It also threatens public safety by reducing our police forces at a time when crime is already rising. People feel unsafe. They are afraid of what criminals will do when there are even fewer law enforcement officers to respond. And they are worried about people's chances of survival if there aren't enough first responders to help in an emergency. The governor underestimates the potential impact of his actions.

“Coercion, intimidation, threats and public shaming are not tactics a leader should be using against the people. The governor's obvious disdain for those who are choosing to lose their jobs rather than compromise their right to make their own medical decisions is unhelpful. He is dividing the public – segregating us and turning people against each other. Yesterday, even unvaccinated health-care workers were heroes. Today, they become villains in the governor's narrative."

Read the full statement. (https://jtwilcox.houserepublicans.wa.gov/2021/10/18/republican-leaders-todays-vaccine-deadline-threatens-critical-services-public-safety/?fbclid=IwAR3WxI9H29hbbmfbZhdkDS9QjqdVd2TIRCdjNjUkUlLdeThtbUjK_WBWJuw)
Smiley Campaign Shows Grassroots Support In Latest Fundraising Quarter
Tiffany Smiley is fighting hard to retire Patty Murray and her latest fundraising numbers show not only has Tiffany raised more than $1.6 million but she also has unequivocal grassroots support, with over 10,000 donors from Washington State. Flipping a U.S. Senate seat is hard work but Tiffany has shown in the only 5 months since starting her campaign, that she is a force to be reckoned with. Learn more about how Tiffany stands on the issues here (https://www.smileyforwashington.com/issues) .
Contact:
Steve Johnson
Communications Director
Washington State Republican Party
(425) 460-0570
steve@wsrp.org (mailto:steve@wsrp.org)

Gildon On Vaccine Mandates State Senator Chris Gildon (R - Puyallup) joins the podcast this week to discuss vaccine mandates, anti-policing laws, the Long-Term Care Tax, and how he got his start in politics. WAGOP is now on Apple Podcasts and Spotify, follow us and never miss an episode! Listen here. () Republican Leaders Fight Inslee's Mandate Thanks to our Republican leaders in the State House and Senate for fighting back and offering a strong rebuke of Inslee's harmful vaccine mandate. Below is an excerpt of their most recent statement to the press: “We are already experiencing higher prices and empty shelves partially as a result of COVID's shutdowns and workforce shortages. Today's deadline, with its unnecessarily hardline consequences, will only make that worse when hundreds, if not thousands, lose their jobs and are denied unemployment benefits. It also threatens public safety by reducing our police forces at a time when crime is already rising. People feel unsafe. They are afraid of what criminals will do when there are even fewer law enforcement officers to respond. And they are worried about people's chances of survival if there aren't enough first responders to help in an emergency. The governor underestimates the potential impact of his actions. “Coercion, intimidation, threats and public shaming are not tactics a leader should be using against the people. The governor's obvious disdain for those who are choosing to lose their jobs rather than compromise their right to make their own medical decisions is unhelpful. He is dividing the public – segregating us and turning people against each other. Yesterday, even unvaccinated health-care workers were heroes. Today, they become villains in the governor's narrative." Read the full statement. () Smiley Campaign Shows Grassroots Support In Latest Fundraising Quarter Tiffany Smiley is fighting hard to retire Patty Murray and her latest fundraising numbers show not only has Tiffany raised more than $1.6 million but she also has unequivocal grassroots support, with over 10,000 donors from Washington State. Flipping a U.S. Senate seat is hard work but Tiffany has shown in the only 5 months since starting her campaign, that she is a force to be reckoned with. Learn more about how Tiffany stands on the issues here () . Contact: Steve Johnson Communications Director Washington State Republican Party (425) 460-0570 steve@wsrp.org (mailto:steve@wsrp.org) ============================================================

**I grew up the son of hardworking farmworkers.**

In order to afford the cost of college, I walked in the desert heat to different businesses asking them to invest in our community by investing in me.

And I made a promise to those businesses I would come back to the Coachella Valley to serve our community as a physician. **I can’t imagine where I would be if my family or my community didn’t have my back.**

**But I’m worried.** National Republicans are threatening to slam the door shut on the American Dream that uplifts communities like mine.

Their plans to upend crucial student debt relief, implement a 30% national sales tax, and give handouts to the wealthy will make it near-impossible for hard working Americans to get by, much less go beyond their station in life.

**This isn’t an accident, friend. It’s a policy choice.**

It happens when National Republicans would rather work for high dollar donors and corporate Super PACs than their own communities. It happens when they bail out the mega-rich at the expense of hardworking people like my parents.

**I’m sick of it.** I’ve spent my life seeing the people of my community be left out and overlooked by people in power. And I’ve spent my career as an emergency physician and in public service fighting to change that.

[**But I need your help, folks. Together we can defeat the National GOP agenda that aims to shred the American Dream and our best line of defense is our grassroots team. Can you pitch in $10 (or anything you can afford today) toward our 1,000 donation goal today and help strengthen our Democratic agenda?**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/4PfL751D8g4IfLXFw3QfXV5Wo_6TP_ujTmJFoO05AE3qXXBxga9GlnVdvLc2VObKISx9q3hczezXKIybUDoad5yVaA_CImPVuTdzGTYQn8f0bEpl1ylhzbQNCFjgZwH4VGzj3pifuCeHnmIPZqK500dy6iKtFKYFBKG67VElyCRXQkuqb5C7VDEtGt4LP8bv5_hdTeViT_QP4mMuwZ6QQJTj5UBPjLoNOnR4jmvzbJIJedvCifFcM4_hRIbk5Tv3l9YvFt1QuNhAA0wj9cdjocblHTea9bizuPQNge4YZv_xkF2KJAO_xrYnvyrQ6cCQS2voPt0SaSn22PoN3QsG3vxIMANTxq18MAaP-qA-OiKxOKatt3g3OhwtsCBjAdw3l3ZrQKnSHCwZHFJjJbZ8BbSv03RP7u-jT4a5iPInoSTwRRHNJ8ayTE76MRxUxLCE/3tz/KJ01H_noT-SDJgmozz_bJg/h5/cbtj9CZnvYNa8Z7e_cjQT-ClY8Lyr2uYmRCZ_SNun6M)****

clean_body: I’m counting on you to help me fight back | | | Team Ruiz hopes that you and your family are safe and healthy. Public health across the country is our top concern during this unprecedented time. To view the most up to date CDC COVID-19 safety recommendations, And if you have a moment, please read our message below. | I grew up the son of hardworking farmworkers. In order to afford the cost of college, I walked in the desert heat to different businesses asking them to invest in our community by investing in me. And I made a promise to those businesses I would come back to the Coachella Valley to serve our community as a physician. I can’t imagine where I would be if my family or my community didn’t have my back. But I’m worried. National Republicans are threatening to slam the door shut on the American Dream that uplifts communities like mine. Their plans to upend crucial student debt relief, implement a 30% national sales tax, and give handouts to the wealthy will make it near-impossible for hard working Americans to get by, much less go beyond their station in life. This isn’t an accident, friend. It’s a policy choice. It happens when National Republicans would rather work for high dollar donors and corporate Super PACs than their own communities. It happens when they bail out the mega-rich at the expense of hardworking people like my parents. I’m sick of it. I’ve spent my life seeing the people of my community be left out and overlooked by people in power. And I’ve spent my career as an emergency physician and in public service fighting to change that. ** If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: Thank you for being with me in this fight, Dr. Ruiz | | Paid for by Dr. Raul Ruiz For Congress Committee P.O. Box 1566, Indio, CA 92202 Please, consider making a . To make the most powerful lasting impact, make a.** to receive fewer emails. | | | Sent via . To update your email address, change your name or address, or to stop receiving emails from Dr. Raul Ruiz for Congress , please .

name: Cliff Schecter, Blue Amp Action
subject: Sign the petition: Tax excessive corporate profits
body:
Obscene profits
| | | | |
---
| | |
---
| | | _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T7fee5c23-4b8d-454c-95a9-cab4c3375b3b/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._
---
|
---
| |
---
| | | | | Peter, ExxonMobil just announced that it had $56 billion in profits in 2022 alone. That comes out to $6.3 million per hour, or more than $100,000 per minute.

Exxon’s news follows announcements that Pfizer raked in $31.4 billion in profit and Chevron ended 2022 with more than $36 billion in profit.

At a time when millions of Americans are struggling to get by and put food on the table, it’s obscene for big corporations to be price gouging consumers in order to maximize profit.

[**Tell Congress: Pass a windfall profits tax to rein in excessive corporate profits****.**](https://default.salsalabs.org/T562e830f-565f-42ca-adb8-13d2ee9bcfa0/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
|
---
| | | | | | [**SIGN THE PETITION**](https://default.salsalabs.org/T6da450f0-9976-425f-85f1-9bd70217ae21/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| In remarks at the White House last year, Pres. Biden called on oil companies to "act beyond their narrow self-interest." If they don’t, he warned, "they’re going to pay a higher tax on their excess profits and face higher restrictions." "It’s time for these companies to stop war profiteering, meet their responsibilities in this country and give the American people a break," Pres. Biden continued. Now, as more and more evidence comes in showing that companies like Exxon, Chevron and Pfizer are greedily taking advantage of consumers, it’s time for Democrats to move forward with a windfall profits tax on big corporations. [**Tell Congress: Pass a windfall profits tax to rein in excessive corporate profits****.**](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tbbb1b605-bceb-4e0d-9b37-e3d09909e882/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) Cliff

| | | About BlueAmp Action: We cannot sugarcoat the current situation. Right-wing authoritarianism, defeated by a worldwide coalition during WWII, was only in hibernation. Over the past decade it has infected democratic societies--including ours. It is as simple as it is terrifying: What we do now will define the future of our country and world. We at Blue Amp Action are dedicated to liberal, open societies, where reason and science are applied to solve our problems; where civil rights--including voting rights!--are protected for all. We fight for these values every day. Speaking up on our platforms, raising the voices and the political prospects of liberal pols and pundits. **And savvy grassroots activists like you know, just like we do, that we can’t stop watching the people we help elect:_ after the elections, we have to keep organizing support for the policies we like and pushing back against the ones we don’t._** **__When the public tunes out between elections and the only people our elected officials hear from are the paid lobbyists of wealthy corporations, our government stops being accountable to the needs of ordinary families.__** **Democracy never sleeps, and neither can we.** So we ask that you support our work, so we can continue to engage in this fight every moment of every day. So our kids grow up in democracy, not dystopia. Thank you for your support for all we do! Please follow Cliff Schecter on Twitter @cliffschecter, or subscribe to The Takedown with Cliff Schecter on YouTube, to keep up to date with this battle for the heart and soul of our country. — _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/Td435243c-6294-4a5d-a5df-e367e6361444/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._

Derek- Earlier this month, the GOP Majority in the House passed to Resolutions of Disapproval that would reverse the most recent overhaul of the DC Criminal Code and the allowance for non-citizens to vote in DC elections. While it came as no surprise that Republicans would oppose the rights of the DC Residents, 47 of the House Democrats joined them in voting for one or both of these measures. In our effort to continue to support DC Statehood, we condemn the actions of these 47 House Democrats and urge our US Senators to reject these resolutions.
---

Council, but also counters the will of the majority of the District's nearly 700,000 residents. We believe that the House of Representatives' actions clearly violate the principles of democratic governance and the fundamental rights of D.C. residents. We want to emphasize that the residents of D.C. were without voting representation during both votes. Moreover, the House never heard from any resident of D.C. as the Mayor's access to the House floor was revoked under the new GOP rules package. This clearly violates the democratic process and the right to representation that all American citizens are entitled to. Join us in our call on the US Senate to reject these resolutions of disapproval and to continue to advocate for the people of the District of Columbia to gain full democratic representation, including granting statehood to the District and ensuring that its residents have voting representation in both houses of Congress.

subject: Setting a goal to raise $20,000

Welcome to the Indiana Democratic Party’s weekly newsletter, The Kernel of Truth. The State Party will provide the most important fact - a kernel of truth - from the week.
**** _INDEMS Stands with LGBTQ Hoosiers Against Discriminatory Bills_ This week, INDems across the state stood with LGBTQ Hoosiers and other advocacy groups as they rallied at the Statehouse to protest the record number of discriminatory bills introduced by the Republican supermajority that target the LGBTQ community. **[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59202130/393553605/620391022?s=20&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1YTRhMzE0NC04YmI0LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=V9Jg63vlQgBzLZz5d_BtXbY_dua08868dr2lEeFNesM=&emci=4b77ce6b-79b4-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&emdi=5a4a3144-8bb4-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&ceid=4925535)****[Share INDems](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59202131/393553606/620391022?s=20&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1YTRhMzE0NC04YmI0LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=V9Jg63vlQgBzLZz5d_BtXbY_dua08868dr2lEeFNesM=&emci=4b77ce6b-79b4-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&emdi=5a4a3144-8bb4-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&ceid=4925535)** The bill known as [HB 1608](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59202132/393553607/1583905928?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1YTRhMzE0NC04YmI0LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=V9Jg63vlQgBzLZz5d_BtXbY_dua08868dr2lEeFNesM=&emci=4b77ce6b-79b4-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&emdi=5a4a3144-8bb4-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&ceid=4925535), or the "Don't Say Gay" bill, aims to forbid LGBTQ topics from being discussed in public schools, effectively eradicating the visibility and perspectives of LGBTQ individuals. The Republican supermajority made a last-minute change to the bill, directing its focus on pressuring teachers to disclose the transgender status of their students to parents. These types of bills not only promote discrimination, but also constitute an assault on the fundamental human rights of an already marginalized community. Indiana isn’t Indiana without LGBTQ Hoosiers, and it is imperative to ensure their equal rights and protection under the law. Unfortunately, the Indiana Republicans have a long history of attacking the LGBTQ community and marginalized in our state, and these bills are just the latest examples of their discriminatory and harmful actions. Indiana Democrats urges all Hoosiers to stand with the LGBTQ community and make their voices heard against discrimination and the record number of anti-LGBTQ bills. Hoosiers who care about responsible investing and the long-term sustainability of our state. At a time when Hoosiers are facing significant financial challenges, the INGOP should be working to support our communities and create opportunities for economic growth. This means investing in job creation, infrastructure improvements, and education, not playing politics with our state's pension funds or schools. INDems called on the INGOP to refocus its efforts on the issues that matter most to Hoosiers. We must work together to build a brighter future for our state, and that means investing in our people and our communities. It is time for the INGOP to put aside its culture wars and work with Democrats to create a better Indiana for all Hoosiers. **Opportunities with INDems** Operations Coordinator **T** The Indiana Democratic Party (IDP) is seeking an Operations Coordinator to help and support our team. In working with the Chair and Executive Director, the Coordinator will oversee the office, run payroll and oversee benefits, oversee IT and office equipment, accounts, and files. They will also be involved in event planning and execution and ensure that the party and its staff and projects all run smoothly. **O** Overall Duties:
* Oversees operations of the office and ensures campaigns and committees have productive workspaces. Manages the relationships with building and parking companies.
* Works on overall organization of filing both physical and digital (recordkeeping) and ensures best practices for IT and security.
* Oversees phone system, internet, printer, and supplies to ensure proper functioning.
* Responsible for onboarding of all new staff and helps current staff navigate any issues with pay, benefits, schedules, etc.
* Point person for health benefits, 401(k), insurance, payroll administrator, reimbursements, travel / logistics, etc.
* Takes the lead on event planning and execution in partnership with the Executive Director and other team members:
* Tours and reviews spaces for events.
* Works with caterers, vendors, and sound and stage consultants.
* Responsible for successful operations with annual dinner, biennial conventions, party retreat, annual holiday party, and other party functions.
* Responsible for establishing coordinated field offices, including securing space, obtaining proper liability insurance, and utilities.

Qualifications: Minimum of at least two (2) years with campaign experience or similar. Experience working in operations or customer service is also preferred.
* Good verbal, written, and analytical skills.
* Experience following and updating budgets.
* Ability to manage several tasks and projects at the same time.
* Ability to prioritize and multi-task in a fast-paced environment.
* Detail oriented and good follow through on tasks.
* Strong communication and interpersonal skills with the ability to interact professionally with staff and external stakeholders and represent the organization with professionalism.
* Ability to maintain a high level of confidentiality.

Salary: Salary is $50,000 per annum. Position includes full medical, dental, and vision insurance benefits, a 401(k) account, and monthly tech stipend. Position is part of the staff union. Application Instructions: Please email your resume and three professional references to jobs@indems.org with “Operations Coordinator” in the subject line. Thank you for your interest in working with IDP.
* * *
## **Executive Director**
The Indiana Democratic Party (IDP) is seeking an Executive Director (ED) to lead our team. In partnership with the Chair and State Central Committee, the ED is responsible for deploying the strategy, overall management, and growth of IDP. The ED will be involved in all aspects of the Party’s operations, including but not limited to: election activities, voter protection, fundraising, budgeting, staff recruitment and retention, outreach, and building party infrastructure. General Duties:** **
* Responsible for overall operational efficiency of IDP, including staff and creation and execution of Party strategy.
* Recruits, interviews, hires, and manages IDP and coordinated campaign staff.
* Manages day-to-day work of Communications, Data & Organizing, Operations, and Finance teams.
* Oversees the day-to-day work of outside consultants and vendors.
* Serves as one of the spokespeople for IDP to the media.
* Other responsibilities as required.

Party Affairs
* Coordinates work and materials for the Democratic State Central Committee, District leaders, County leaders, and precinct committeemen and women.
* Keeps up strong relationships with various constituencies and stakeholders around the state, including organized labor, African-American leaders, Latino leaders, LGBTQ Hoosiers, women’s groups, Indiana Young Democrats, and College Democrats.
* Maintains close working relationships with various party partner organizations including, but not limited to: DNC, ASDC, DCCC, DSCC, DGA, and DLCC.
* Builds and nurtures relationships with Democratic candidates, elected officials, Democratic caucuses, and staff members across the state.
* Supports the building, growth, and success of county parties throughout the state.
* Oversees an annual party retreat, annual fundraising dinner, biennial state conventions, and the National Delegate Selection process for the 2024 Democratic convention.

Campaign & Election Activities
* Shares regular organizing, training, coaching, and other resources online and in-person for Party staff, volunteers, and party leadership.
* Works with IDP and Indiana Election Division to ensure the Party is up-to-date on the current and latest rules relating to elections and voting in Indiana.
* Troubleshoots any issues with campaigns or county parties on behalf of IDP to ensure the Party is represented well and that issues are resolved in a timely fashion.** **
* Oversees voter protection in-state and in partnership with DNC guidance.
* Oversees GOTV and Election Day efforts and events for IDP.

Fundraising
* Works with the State Chair to raise money for the party through call time, meetings, events, memberships, online giving, and other income generating streams.
* Partners with the finance team to create a robust apparatus for fundraising and cultivating large and small donors.
* Represents IDP at party, media, or community functions across the state.

Operations & Compliance
* Manages office and ensures campaigns and committees have productive workspaces.
* Keeps an updated and working knowledge of campaign finance, election, and compliance laws and works in concert with the compliance team and legal counsel to ensure all Party activities are consistently compliant.
* Works with the State Chair and Party CPA and Treasurer to implement the budget.

Qualifications: Minimum of at least four (4) years with direct campaign experience. Executive experience working on a coordinated campaign, statewide or Congressional political campaign, or with a state political party preferred.
* Experience managing a growing, diverse, and highly skilled team, with demonstrated ability to achieve high levels of performance and outcomes through guidance and management.
* A reputation for leading with integrity and making decisions with the input of the appropriate stakeholders.
* Experience building, managing, and tracking large budgets.
* Excellent verbal, written, and analytical skills.
* Ability to manage several tasks and projects at the same time.
* Ability to prioritize and multi-task in a fast-paced environment.
* Detail oriented and good follow through on tasks.
* Strong communication and interpersonal skills with the ability to interact professionally with staff and external stakeholders and represent the organization with professionalism.
* Ability to maintain a high level of confidentiality.

Salary:
Salary is generally competitive and commensurate with experience. Position includes full medical, dental, and vision insurance benefits, a 401(k) account, and monthly tech stipend. Application Instructions:
Please email your resume and three professional references to [jobs@indems.org](mailto:jobs@indems.org) with “Executive Director” in the subject line. Thank you for your interest in working with IDP.

clean_body: | Welcome to the Indiana Democratic Party’s weekly newsletter, The Kernel of Truth. The State Party will provide the most important fact - a kernel of truth - from the week. ** INDEMS Stands with LGBTQ Hoosiers Against Discriminatory Bills This week, INDems across the state stood with LGBTQ Hoosiers and other advocacy groups as they rallied at the Statehouse to protest the record number of discriminatory bills introduced by the Republican supermajority that target the LGBTQ community. The bill known as , or the "Don't Say Gay" bill, aims to forbid LGBTQ topics from being discussed in public schools, effectively eradicating the visibility and perspectives of LGBTQ individuals. The Republican supermajority made a last-minute change to the bill, directing its focus on pressuring teachers to disclose the transgender status of their students to parents. These types of bills not only promote discrimination, but also constitute an assault on the fundamental human rights of an already marginalized community. Indiana isn’t Indiana without LGBTQ Hoosiers, and it is imperative to ensure their equal rights and protection under the law. Unfortunately, the Indiana Republicans have a long history of attacking the LGBTQ community and marginalized in our state, and these bills are just the latest examples of their discriminatory and harmful actions. Indiana Democrats urges all Hoosiers to stand with the LGBTQ community and make their voices heard against discrimination and the record number of anti-LGBTQ bills. See INDems’ release by . **Anti-ESG Bill is Latest Example of INGOP’s Neverending Culture War The Republican supermajority approved an bill out of the Indiana House this week with a new price tag of $5.5 million over the next decade is a prime example of this misplaced focus. The anti-ESG bill, which seeks to prohibit state pension funds from investing in companies that prioritize environmental, social, and governance factors, is a direct attack on the values of many Hoosiers who care about responsible investing and the long-term sustainability of our state. At a time when Hoosiers are facing significant financial challenges, the INGOP should be working to support our communities and create opportunities for economic growth. This means investing in job creation, infrastructure improvements, and education, not playing politics with our state's pension funds or schools. INDems called on the INGOP to refocus its efforts on the issues that matter most to Hoosiers. We must work together to build a brighter future for our state, and that means investing in our people and our communities. It is time for the INGOP to put aside its culture wars and work with Democrats to create a better Indiana for all Hoosiers. See INDems’ full release by . **Each week, the State Party will highlight key policy from Democrats at the Statehouse or across the state. Democrats deliver a better future for Hoosier families, and we urge you to share this information with your networks. State Representative Mitch Gore passed a bill out of the Indiana House this week that would strengthen a ban on devices that turn firearms into machine guns. Rep. Gore said the following after its passage out of committee, “This legislation will make our police and our communities safer. These conversion devices allow shooters to fire off more rounds at a much faster rate...By adding these devices to the definition of 'machine gun,' those found in unlawful possession would face up to a level 5 felony. With that deterrent in place, we'll hopefully see a decrease in the number of fatal and non-fatal shootings in Indiana.” now heads to the Indiana Senate to be considered. **** NDTC & Indiana Democratic Party Blue Bench Training Join the National Democratic Training Committee & Indiana Democratic Party on Saturday, February 25th from 9-4 pm ET for a free, in-person Blue Bench training. If you’re running for office, working on a campaign, or working with your local Democratic Party, this training will provide the tools, skills, and insight you need to succeed. During this full-day training, you’ll: - Build the foundational skills needed to run a successful campaign or strengthen your community’s Democratic infrastructure. - Learn from campaign experts and gain key insight to guide your efforts in 2023. - Connect with leaders across Indiana to network, support, and learn from each other’s work. - Understand how to access on-going resources to support your goals. 2023 IDEA Convention: Save the Date! ** INDems IN the News : Indiana senators not so keen on "school choice" voucher expansion in House-proposed budget : Expert: Indiana's proposed budget needs more on public health : A new slogan for ambitious Indiana politicians : Republicans back bill for tighter Indiana mail voting rules : Anti-trans medical care bill passes Senate committee : Indiana Attorney General Todd Rokita seeking reelection in 2024 : Indiana Senate approves bill criminalizing protests outside people's homes : Indiana lawmakers advancing bills to crack down on gun modifications : Tenants and housing advocates will have to wait — again : House committee passes bill aimed at improving mental health of officers : Don't Say Gay, first responder mental health, Republican budget, climate task force : Democrats propose budget alternatives as spending bill advances to House floor : Indiana 'Don't Say Gay' bill pivots to kids' gender identity : Bill to allow teachers to be armed passes Indiana House : State budget: House GOP goes big on education, cutting taxes, pulls back on health dollars : Indiana lawmakers to discuss bill similar to Florida's 'Don't Say Gay' law Monday : Indiana bill that would test firefighters for toxic PFAS levels passes through House : Indiana House Republicans propose major school voucher expansion in next state budget Opportunities with INDems Operations Coordinator T The Indiana Democratic Party (IDP) is seeking an Operations Coordinator to help and support our team. In working with the Chair and Executive Director, the Coordinator will oversee the office, run payroll and oversee benefits, oversee IT and office equipment, accounts, and files. They will also be involved in event planning and execution and ensure that the party and its staff and projects all run smoothly. O Overall Duties: Oversees operations of the office and ensures campaigns and committees have productive workspaces. Manages the relationships with building and parking companies. Works on overall organization of filing both physical and digital (recordkeeping) and ensures best practices for IT and security. Oversees phone system, internet, printer, and supplies to ensure proper functioning. Responsible for onboarding of all new staff and helps current staff navigate any issues with pay, benefits, schedules, etc. Point person for health benefits, 401(k), insurance, payroll administrator, reimbursements, travel / logistics, etc. Takes the lead on event planning and execution in partnership with the Executive Director and other team members: Tours and reviews spaces for events. Works with caterers, vendors, and sound and stage consultants. Responsible for successful operations with annual dinner, biennial conventions, party retreat, annual holiday party, and other party functions. Responsible for establishing coordinated field offices, including securing space, obtaining proper liability insurance, and utilities. Qualifications: Minimum of at least two (2) years with campaign experience or similar. Experience working in operations or customer service is also preferred. Good verbal, written, and analytical skills. Experience following and updating budgets. Ability to manage several tasks and projects at the same time. Ability to prioritize and multi-task in a fast-paced environment. Detail oriented and good follow through on tasks. Strong communication and interpersonal skills with the ability to interact professionally with staff and external stakeholders and represent the organization with professionalism. Ability to maintain a high level of confidentiality. Salary: Salary is $50,000 per annum. Position includes full medical, dental, and vision insurance benefits, a 401(k) account, and monthly tech stipend. Position is part of the staff union. Application Instructions: Please email your resume and three professional references to jobs@indems.org with “Operations Coordinator” in the subject line. Thank you for your interest in working with IDP. Executive Director The Indiana Democratic Party (IDP) is seeking an Executive Director (ED) to lead our team. In partnership with the Chair and State Central Committee, the ED is responsible for deploying the strategy, overall management, and growth of IDP. The ED will be involved in all aspects of the Party’s operations, including but not limited to: election activities, voter protection, fundraising, budgeting, staff recruitment and retention, outreach, and building party infrastructure. General Duties: Responsible for overall operational efficiency of IDP, including staff and creation and execution of Party strategy. Recruits, interviews, hires, and manages IDP and coordinated campaign staff. Manages day-to-day work of Communications, Data & Organizing, Operations, and Finance teams. Oversees the day-to-day work of outside consultants and vendors. Serves as one of the spokespeople for IDP to the media. Other responsibilities as required. Party Affairs Coordinates work and materials for the Democratic State Central Committee, District leaders, County leaders, and precinct committeemen and women. Keeps up strong relationships with various constituencies and stakeholders around the state, including organized labor, African-American leaders, Latino leaders, LGBTQ Hoosiers, women’s groups, Indiana Young Democrats, and College Democrats. Maintains close working relationships with various party partner organizations including, but not limited to: DNC, ASDC, DCCC, DSCC, DGA, and DLCC. Builds and nurtures relationships with Democratic candidates, elected officials, Democratic caucuses, and staff members across the state. Supports the building, growth, and success of county parties throughout the state. Oversees an annual party retreat, annual fundraising dinner, biennial state conventions, and the National Delegate Selection process for the 2024 Democratic convention. Campaign & Election Activities Shares regular organizing, training, coaching, and other resources online and in-person for Party staff, volunteers, and party leadership. Works with IDP and Indiana Election Division to ensure the Party is up-to-date on the current and latest rules relating to elections and voting in Indiana. Troubleshoots any issues with campaigns or county parties on behalf of IDP to ensure the Party is represented well and that issues are resolved in a timely fashion. Oversees voter protection in-state and in partnership with DNC guidance. Oversees GOTV and Election Day efforts and events for IDP. Fundraising Works with the State Chair to raise money for the party through call time, meetings, events, memberships, online giving, and other income generating streams. Partners with the finance team to create a robust apparatus for fundraising and cultivating large and small donors. Represents IDP at party, media, or community functions across the state. Operations & Compliance Manages office and ensures campaigns and committees have productive workspaces. Keeps an updated and working knowledge of campaign finance, election, and compliance laws and works in concert with the compliance team and legal counsel to ensure all Party activities are consistently compliant. Works with the State Chair and Party CPA and Treasurer to implement the budget. Qualifications: Minimum of at least four (4) years with direct campaign experience. Executive experience working on a coordinated campaign, statewide or Congressional political campaign, or with a state political party preferred. Experience managing a growing, diverse, and highly skilled team, with demonstrated ability to achieve high levels of performance and outcomes through guidance and management. A reputation for leading with integrity and making decisions with the input of the appropriate stakeholders. Experience building, managing, and tracking large budgets. Excellent verbal, written, and analytical skills. Ability to manage several tasks and projects at the same time. Ability to prioritize and multi-task in a fast-paced environment. Detail oriented and good follow through on tasks. Strong communication and interpersonal skills with the ability to interact professionally with staff and external stakeholders and represent the organization with professionalism. * Ability to maintain a high level of confidentiality. Salary: Salary is generally competitive and commensurate with experience. Position includes full medical, dental, and vision insurance benefits, a 401(k) account, and monthly tech stipend. Application Instructions: Please email your resume and three professional references to mailto:jobs@indems.org with “Executive Director” in the subject line. Thank you for your interest in working with IDP. [content goes here] Indiana Democratic Party 115 W Washington INDLPS, IN 46202 United States Paid for by the Indiana Democratic Party, www.indems.org. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee.

name: Team AOC
subject: East Palestine
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress
body:
# [](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/143?refcode=em20230224-3933-header&t=1&akid=3933%2E2526796%2ETGtF5A)
Earlier this month, a 149-car freight train carrying hazardous materials derailed in the small town of East Palestine, Ohio spilling over 100,000 gallons of dangerous chemicals.1
In an attempt by authorities to prevent an explosion at the derailment site, the chemicals were later burned — sending a huge plume of toxic black smoke over East Palestine.2
[
](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20230224.qUhgfe&refcode=em20230224-3933-P_FND_CHA_DIR&amounts=6%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=2&akid=3933%2E2526796%2ETGtF5A)_Gene J. Puskar / NBC News_
In the two weeks since the accident, residents have reported headaches, dizziness, nausea, and bloody noses and fear the long-term effects on their families and children.3 Our movement is not standing on the sidelines — **we must come together to provide mutual aid support to the East Palestine community. We’ve identified three organizations providing direct assistance and relief to families in Ohio affected by the toxic derailment.**
[Can you make a $3 split contribution today to support direct relief in East Palestine, Ohio? 100% of your contribution will go directly to these three organizations:](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20230224.qUhgfe&refcode=em20230224-3933-P_FND_CHA_DIR&amounts=6%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=3&akid=3933%2E2526796%2ETGtF5A)
[The Way Station](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/16861?t=4&akid=3933%2E2526796%2ETGtF5A) is a community resource for families and individuals in need. They have responded to the crisis in East Palestine by providing food, clothing, water, hygiene products, diapers, cleaning supplies and gift cards to those in need.
[The Brightside Project](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/16862?t=5&akid=3933%2E2526796%2ETGtF5A) is a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization based in Columbiana County that normally focuses its efforts on low-income and underserved families. Since the East Palestine train derailment, the organization has shifted gears to focus on the families and children directly affected by the train derailment.
[River Valley Organizing](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/16863?t=6&akid=3933%2E2526796%2ETGtF5A) is a multi-racial, multicultural, working class organization that radically builds community throughout the Ohio River Valley. They perform desperately-needed service work and organize the political power of everyday people, centering the leadership of the most affected community members. Currently they are focused on organizing the residents of East Palestine to hold Norfolk Southern accountable for the derailment.
**[Contribute $3](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20230224.qUhgfe&refcode=em20230224-3933-P_FND_CHA_DIR&amounts=6%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=25&akid=3933%2E2526796%2ETGtF5A)**
Residents have also reported losing pets to sudden illness. And as many as 45,000 animals — mainly fish, have reportedly died as a result of the spill.2
We won’t know the full health or environmental toll of this accident for years, perhaps decades. But we do know that the residents of East Palestine, Ohio need support _now_.
[If you can, please make a $3 contribution to help provide direct assistance to families affected by the toxic train derailment.](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20230224.qUhgfe&refcode=em20230224-3933-P_FND_CHA_DIR&amounts=6%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=26&akid=3933%2E2526796%2ETGtF5A)
Make no mistake, this accident is the direct result of deregulation under the Trump administration and poor safety standards. Labor unions have been sounding the alarm that making major cuts to the railroad workforce, increasing train lengths, and reducing inspection times have made trains less safe.4
Alexandria will continue to keep up the pressure on corporate CEOs and private firms like Norfolk Southern to do what is needed to bring our rail system into the 21st century.
Thank you for your attention and support,
Team AOC
* * *
1 - [NBC News](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/17452?t=27&akid=3933%2E2526796%2ETGtF5A)
2 - [BBC](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/17453?t=28&akid=3933%2E2526796%2ETGtF5A)
3 - [CNN](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/17454?t=29&akid=3933%2E2526796%2ETGtF5A)
4 - [USA Today](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/17455?t=30&akid=3933%2E2526796%2ETGtF5A)

**[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/aoc-email-20230224?refcode=em20230224-3933-P_FND_CHA_DIR&t=31&refcode2=3933_2526796_TGtF5A&akid=3933%2E2526796%2ETGtF5A)** | **[Merch](https://shop.ocasiocortez.com/?utm_source=em-footer-20230224-3933-P_FND_CHA_DIR&t=32&akid=3933%2E2526796%2ETGtF5A)**
---|---
[](https://www.facebook.com/OcasioCortez/?t=33&akid=3933%2E2526796%2ETGtF5A) | [](https://twitter.com/AOC?t=34&akid=3933%2E2526796%2ETGtF5A) | [](https://www.instagram.com/aoc/?t=35&akid=3933%2E2526796%2ETGtF5A)
---|---|---
Email us: [us@ocasiocortez.com](mailto: us@ocasiocortez.com)
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, so thank you for reading to the end. Small donors like you keep us going. To contribute via check, please address to Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, PO Box 680080, Corona, NY 11368.
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/)
* [Update My Personal Information](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/signup/did-your-information-change/?t=36&akid=3933%2E2526796%2ETGtF5A)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=37&akid=3933%2E2526796%2ETGtF5A)

Sure, raising $12,000 in the next four days is a little ambitious. But the only reason I’ve set our goal this high is because I know what this team is capable of.
You helped us raise a historic amount of funds in the previous election – all of which went to defeating my far-right opponent.
Now, as we prepare for an even tougher fight in 2024, we can’t afford to come up short on a single fundraising deadline. _And believe me, keeping my seat blue is going to take every last dollar we raise._
Kevin McCarthy and his allies will funnel millions of dark money into Nevada to flip this seat and hold on to his House Majority by a thread. Thankfully, I’m fighting like hell to make sure that doesn’t happen. But I need your support now just as I did in 2022.
[**Can I count on you to help my campaign reach its biggest goal since the election by chipping in right now? clean_body: | = | | = Look, folks — I have to give it to you straight. February is the shortest month of the year but we still have to raise the same amount as any other month. So, I’m setting our end-of-month goal for February at $12,000 – our largest since the election. This is where I’m relying on your support: = | | | = Sure, raising $12,000 in the next four days is a little ambitious. But the only reason I’ve set our goal this high is because I know what this team is capable of. You helped us raise a historic amount of funds in the previous election – all of which went to defeating my far-right opponent. Now, as we prepare for an even tougher fight in 2024, we can’t afford to come up short on a single fundraising deadline. And believe me, keeping my seat blue is going to take every last dollar we raise. Kevin McCarthy and his allies will funnel millions of dark money into Nevada to flip this seat and hold on to his House Majority by a thread. Thankfully, I’m fighting like hell to make sure that doesn’t happen. But I need your support now just as I did in 2022. = | | = | | = | | = | | = | | = | | = Let’s get it done! name: Jon Tester
subject: I’m just a dirt farmer
disclaimer_text: Paid for by BACKROADS PAC, and not authorized by any candidate or any candidate's committee
body:
I’m pretty different from most people who end up in the U.S. Senate.

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59202563/393554671/1999187487?refcode=BLU-BRP-20230224-z-EM&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83ODM2NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhNDE5YzkyZi04ZGI0LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=X0H5Czcv8s80OF8skYWgJQni6j2kym4fDcUTtpMizU8=)
---
Peter, can I tell you a little story about me?

I grew up on my family’s farm and it’s still home today. That explains a lot about me.

When I was a kid, I lost three fingers on my left hand while working in our family’s butcher shop. I don’t even remember putting my hand there — but I can damn sure remember rushing to a local doctor.

Here’s why I’m telling you this: just like my $12 flattop haircut from my local barbershop back home and my collection of dirt-stained t-shirts, **my background is a big part of the reason I remain grounded and focused amid the partisan politics and nonsense happening in our government.**

With all the chaos in Washington (especially now), you need somebody who will fight to protect our public lands, create good-paying jobs, and ensure rural communities have hospitals within driving distance in case of an emergency.

**I’m pretty different from most people who end up in the U.S. Senate. I’m just a dirt farmer trying to do what’s right for the people in my state and across the country.**

[_**I’m officially running for reelection, so here’s the part where I ask: Can you become a Founding Donor of my campaign to help us block Mitch McConnell from retaking the Senate?**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59202564/393554672/1999187487?refcode=BLU-BRP-20230224-z-EM&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83ODM2NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhNDE5YzkyZi04ZGI0LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=X0H5Czcv8s80OF8skYWgJQni6j2kym4fDcUTtpMizU8=)
| [DONATE ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59202565/393554673/1999187487?refcode=BLU-BRP-20230224-z-EM&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83ODM2NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhNDE5YzkyZi04ZGI0LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=X0H5Czcv8s80OF8skYWgJQni6j2kym4fDcUTtpMizU8=)
---
You don’t need ten fingers to know right from wrong and you can bet I’m going to spend every single day trying to do right by working families, farmers, students, and veterans here in Montana and across the country.

I appreciate your support, and anything you can pitch in today will go a long way for our grassroots campaign.

Thanks again,

Jon

clean_body: I’m pretty different from most people who end up in the U.S. Senate. | = Peter, can I tell you a little story about me? I grew up on my family’s farm and it’s still home today. That explains a lot about me. When I was a kid, I lost three fingers on my left hand while working in our family’s butcher shop. I don’t even remember putting my hand there — but I can damn sure remember rushing to a local doctor. Here’s why I’m telling you this: just like my $12 flattop haircut from my local barbershop back home and my collection of dirt-stained t-shirts, my background is a big part of the reason I remain grounded and focused amid the partisan politics and nonsense happening in our government. With all the chaos in Washington (especially now), you need somebody who will fight to protect our public lands, create good-paying jobs, and ensure rural communities have hospitals within driving distance in case of an emergency. I’m pretty different from most people who end up in the U.S. Senate. I’m just a dirt farmer trying to do what’s right for the people in my state and across the country. = | = You don’t need ten fingers to know right from wrong and you can bet I’m going to spend every single day trying to do right by working families, farmers, students, and veterans here in Montana and across the country. I appreciate your support, and anything you can pitch in today will go a long way for our grassroots campaign. Thanks again, Jon * | | = Paid for by BACKROADS PAC, and not authorized by any candidate or any candidate's committee. BACKROADS PAC PO Box 713 Canton, NY 13617 United States | = = BACKROADS PAC is an organization dedicated to giving rural Democrats the resources to run for office and defeat Republicans everywhere. Republicans are threatening once again to cut Social Security and Medicare – essential services that millions of Americans rely on.

name: JusticeDemocrats.com
subject: Complete transparency
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS
body:
[1]Justice Democrats

Peter,

We’re going to be completely transparent with you about the state of our
fundraising this month.

Here’s a snapshot right from our ActBlue dashboard:

We’re proud that our average contribution is just $16.74 because it proves
that Justice Democrats is a grassroots-powered organization.

But because we’re grassroots-funded, we have to hit every single
fundraising goal that we set. But, over the last year, we’ve seen a
continued decline in our fundraising and in order to avoid budget cuts for
this quarter, we need to raise $35,000 before midnight on the 28th.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/128337?refcode=em20230224-27700&t=1001&akid=27700%2E1203867%2EPGZtw1 ]Peter, will you
make your first
contribution of $3 right now to help make sure we have the
resources to recruit more candidates like Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez to run
for Congress?

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/128337?refcode=em20230224-27700&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1020&akid=27700%2E1203867%2EPGZtw1 ]Contribute $3

Imagine the Democratic Party without AOC, Jamaal Bowman, or Cori Bush in
Congress. Justice Democrats recruited each of these leaders to run and win
and we’re building the infrastructure for even more grassroots candidates
to do the same.

But we’re facing millions of dollars in attacks and spending from groups
like AIPAC and Moderate PAC who want to stop us from recruiting and
electing our next slate of progressive champions.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/128337?refcode=em20230224-27700&t=1021&akid=27700%2E1203867%2EPGZtw1 ]Please, if you can afford it, make your best gift right now to help us
reach our February end-of-month fundraising goal before the deadline.

Thank you for everything you’re doing to support our movement,

Justice Democrats

Do not worry if you cannot afford to make a contribution — we understand that
this is a difficult time. If you’re struggling, you can find a food bank
[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/28883?t=1022&akid=27700%2E1203867%2EPGZtw1 ]here. We appreciate everything you do to keep our movement strong.

Please stay informed and follow the most up-to-date recommendations from the
[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/24438?t=1023&akid=27700%2E1203867%2EPGZtw1 ]Centers for Disease Control and your state public health department.

Contribute to Justice Democrats: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/justicedemocrats?refcode=em20230224-27700-footer&t=1024&refcode2=27700_1203867_PGZtw1&akid=27700%2E1203867%2EPGZtw1

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://go.justicedemocrats.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1025&akid=27700%2E1203867%2EPGZtw1

name: Team Jayapal
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Pramila for Congress
body:
#
Peter, **_it’s unbelievable._**
While you’ve been paying the price at the pump, Big Oil is raking in record profits. **They’ve been price gouging millions of customers** and calling it “inflation” — and they’re getting away with it.
These companies are shameless in their extortion, all for the sake of lining their stakeholders’ pockets at the expense of everyone else. Combined, BP, Shell, and Exxon made **_$123,600,000,000_** in 2022.
[](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/38321?refcode=em20230224-2208&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=2208%2E420207%2E1Y5QJr)
But here’s the kicker: **every single House Republican voted _against_ ending price gouging just like this.**
[**It’s clear that Republicans are unwilling to put our communities ahead of their own wallets — which is why Pramila has fought for economic and environmental justice at every turn. Can you chip in $3 to support her today?**](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/38321?refcode=em20230224-2208&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=2&akid=2208%2E420207%2E1Y5QJr)
**[Contribute $3](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/38321?refcode=em20230224-2208&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=21&akid=2208%2E420207%2E1Y5QJr)**
While the GOP blames Democrats for the high cost of necessities like gas and groceries, they’ve blocked every effort we’ve made to help working people get by. Instead, they give handouts to industries like Big Oil. It’s despicable.
Our forests are burning. Our towns are flooding. Severe storms have incapacitated our cities. And what do corporations like Shell, Exxon, and BP do? They double down on their business plan to wreak havoc on our climate and come up with new ways to rip us off. **Oil companies have known about climate change for decades and done nothing to stop it** -- they’ll do whatever it takes to make a profit, and Republicans will let them.
But Pramila isn’t letting Big Oil get away with it. She’s been fighting for a Green New Deal since it was first introduced in the House in 2018. As Chair of the Progressive Caucus, Pramila is leading the charge for climate and environmental justice. **But she can’t do it alone.**
[**She needs our help to stand up to Big Oil and fight back against the GOP majority. Will you support Pramila today with a donation of $3 or more?**](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/37721?refcode=em20230224-2208&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=22&akid=2208%2E420207%2E1Y5QJr)
In solidarity,
-- Team Jayapal

**[Contribute](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pj-email-footer?refcode=em20230224-2208-footer&t=23&refcode2=2208_420207_1Y5QJr&akid=2208%2E420207%2E1Y5QJr)**
---
[](https://www.facebook.com/pramila.jayapal) | [](https://twitter.com/pramilajayapal) | [](https://www.instagram.com/pramilajayapal/)
Email us: info@pramilaforcongress.com
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, and small-dollar contributions sustain our movement for transformative change. To contribute by check, please address to Pramila for Congress, PO Box 21912, Seattle, WA 98111.
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/)
* [Update My Personal Information](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/signup/did-your-info-change/?t=24&akid=2208%2E420207%2E1Y5QJr)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=25&akid=2208%2E420207%2E1Y5QJr)

Paid for by Pramila for Congress
clean_body: Peter, it’s unbelievable. While you’ve been paying the price at the pump, Big Oil is raking in record profits. They’ve been price gouging millions of customers and calling it “inflation” — and they’re getting away with it. These companies are shameless in their extortion, all for the sake of lining their stakeholders’ pockets at the expense of everyone else. Combined, BP, Shell, and Exxon made $123,600,000,000 in 2022. But here’s the kicker: every single House Republican voted against ending price gouging just like this. While the GOP blames Democrats for the high cost of necessities like gas and groceries, they’ve blocked every effort we’ve made to help working people get by. Instead, they give handouts to industries like Big Oil. It’s despicable. Our forests are burning. Our towns are flooding. Severe storms have incapacitated our cities. And what do corporations like Shell, Exxon, and BP do? They double down on their business plan to wreak havoc on our climate and come up with new ways to rip us off. Oil companies have known about climate change for decades and done nothing to stop it -- they’ll do whatever it takes to make a profit, and Republicans will let them. But Pramila isn’t letting Big Oil get away with it. She’s been fighting for a Green New Deal since it was first introduced in the House in 2018. As Chair of the Progressive Caucus, Pramila is leading the charge for climate and environmental justice. But she can’t do it alone. In solidarity, -- Team Jayapal | | Email us: info@pramilaforcongress.com

name: Tammy Baldwin
subject: Can I share something with you?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Democratic Party of Georgia
body:
|
---

[ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59204507/393559465/227139380?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTA4MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0NDcxZTQzNi05MmI0LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=N70PH6NJWJ-_qZ2QdoIbrXkuuE-jea2V1Is1Nd9lPUg=&emci=5a9d55b3-64b4-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&emdi=4471e436-92b4-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&ceid=43566853)

| | I’m used to being written off, Peter.

When I first ran for Congress in Wisconsin in 1997, no one thought I could win — especially when an extremely popular challenger jumped into the race.

I remember huddling with one of my staffers at the time, and she said, “Well Tammy, maybe you want to rethink what you’re doing because it’s going to be tough.”

I looked her right in the eye and asked, “Do you think I should wait my turn?”

The next thing I said was this: I believe that women have to run — _especially when it’s hard_ — and not wait our turn. We can’t wait until we get a tap on our shoulder or we’ll never get anywhere.

**I ended up winning that race and became the very first woman elected to the House of Representatives from Wisconsin. And it wouldn’t be the last time our team would make history.**

When I ran for U.S. Senate in 2012, an incredible team of grassroots supporters like you fought alongside me — even as some people wrote us off. You helped me become the first woman elected to the chamber from our state and the first openly gay Senator in our nation’s history.

In 2018, despite being a top target for national Republicans and enduring over a year of attacks, you stood with me again, and we won re-election by _double digits._

Time and again, grassroots supporters like you have defied the odds across the country – in Georgia, in Wisconsin, everywhere. The battle to protect this seat in 2024 will be no different, and it will require all of us working together to overcome the millions of dollars right-wing mega-donors will spend in an attempt to defeat us and hand the Senate majority over to Mitch McConnell.

[**_Together, I know we can defy the odds once again. So Peter, I’m humbly asking: Will you help us protect this seat and the Senate majority with it by splitting a donation today between the Democratic Party of Georgia and my campaign in Wisconsin?_**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59204508/393559466/2063583167?refcode=20230224.2_TB_2AS&amount=15&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTA4MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0NDcxZTQzNi05MmI0LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=N70PH6NJWJ-_qZ2QdoIbrXkuuE-jea2V1Is1Nd9lPUg=) | _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_

I’m so glad to have you on this historic team.

— Tammy

name: Hung Cao, me (2)
subject: re: Following up on your invitation
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Unleash America PAC
body:
Hi Peter,
As we close out our founding member $5K donor drive, I wanted to reach out to you one last time.

When we launched Unleash America PAC at the beginning of the month, I never expected we'd come so far in such a short period of time.

Patriots like you stepped up to empower our mission, and now, we're one step closer to bringing real leadership to America's capital.

But even with such a strong start, there's still more work to be done, and our $5K founding donor drive ends **tonight** at **midnight.**

Peter, when I started working with Unleash America PAC, I knew we had to think big.

I want to help elect candidates who will impact our nation in ways we haven't seen in years.

I want real leaders who live by the same values you do every day. Real leaders who exude bravery, patriotism, and morale in everything that they do.

It's the kind of leadership that's been lacking in Washington - and it's the kind of leadership you deserve.

Before we close out our $5K drive, I want to make sure YOU have an opportunity to become a part of this last generation of change makers.

It won't be easy, and it's going to take a lot of resources - but with you on our team, I know we can get it done.

**Click your personal link below to view to become a founding member of Unleash America PAC during our $5K donor drive and join the movement to keep the American Dream alive for the next generation.**
**[https://unleashamericapac.com/Peter](http://links.unleashamericapac.co/u/click?_t=4fd2065bb87d4411bcd7523509144268&_m=5df1a09bce4e49eab25937d39fdd5a90&_e=oHv_dpOKPlr4oBKkbLkDwdFVSz_xH2PUGI41R_03f-z3XYyv1V5NQtREQ-xlc3DMUHyTjawVMP-Z2am9c-_DWxKUhXzBDJG8_5SDJmyRTEKjYQlsha0WaXtxblnTv0Nl6clGajSMvabg8dMa7nEv-juTNVKddoq48Gt6X2L3HrPQIFF8ROIdttoGlBWCp8WmZlEPd0PxBoDQLnlJZ9qp9rZtxBNkRNy6i2mOCHjEsiihB4OGN6XGZ1d7E91hgNJFzeXCipCac-6W29ijhygdaUhGxZ-pLmpiOa-ML8jvZiwtEoIeyx-VsiW5bs2zI2p6)**

In Liberty,

Hung Cao

clean_body: Hi Peter, As we close out our founding member $5K donor drive, I wanted to reach out to you one last time. When we launched Unleash America PAC at the beginning of the month, I never expected we'd come so far in such a short period of time. Patriots like you stepped up to empower our mission, and now, we're one step closer to bringing real leadership to America's capital. But even with such a strong start, there's still more work to be done, and our $5K founding donor drive ends tonight at midnight. Peter, when I started working with Unleash America PAC, I knew we had to think big. I want to help elect candidates who will impact our nation in ways we haven't seen in years. I want real leaders who live by the same values you do every day. Real leaders who exude bravery, patriotism, and morale in everything that they do. It's the kind of leadership that's been lacking in Washington - and it's the kind of leadership you deserve. Before we close out our $5K drive, I want to make sure YOU have an opportunity to become a part of this last generation of change makers. It won't be easy, and it's going to take a lot of resources - but with you on our team, I know we can get it done. Click your personal link below to view to become a founding member of Unleash America PAC during our $5K donor drive and join the movement to keep the American Dream alive for the next generation. In Liberty, Hung Cao Paid for by Unleash America PAC. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate committee. If you would like to be removed from this list, .

Friend,

Ohio doesn't need another Senator kowtowing to D.C. politicians and the party line.

Ohio needs leaders.

Matt Dolan is focused on serving the people of Ohio. He's built a proven conservative record helping Ohioans save their hard-earned money, build businesses, and live free.

**On the other hand -- Sherrod Brown has spent his entire time in the U.S. Senate serving his party's leadership, and he has voted with Joe Biden **98.5%** of the time.**

**Here 's EXACTLY what Sherrod Brown stands for ->**
* Expanding the Supreme Court and packing it with far-left judges.
* Keeping our unsecured southern border wide open.
* Gutting our healthcare system and eliminating private health insurance.
* Ending the filibuster to enforce the Far-Left agenda.
* Undermining American energy independence and Ohio jobs.

Sherrod Brown's blind loyalty is a disservice to the people of this great state. It's time to get Ohio back on track and start putting the **PEOPLE** first.
